title: Financial Analysis Associate
description: **Job Title:** Financial Analysis Associate **Sector:** Financial Services **Location:** Dublin (On\-site) **Salary:** €65,000 \- €75,000 \+ bonus, pension \& healthcare **Our Client** Our client is a leading global financial services organisation currently expanding its Dublin finance team. They are seeking a highly analytical finance professional to join a growing function focused on financial planning, performance analysis and decision support. This is a high\-visibility role offering exposure to senior stakeholders and key business initiatives within a fast\-paced and commercially focused environment. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity for a qualified accountant looking to move into a commercial finance and analysis role within a high\-performing organisation. The successful candidate will gain exposure to budgeting, forecasting, financial modelling and strategic analysis while working closely with senior stakeholders across the business. The role is particularly suited to candidates from Transaction Advisory, Deals Advisory or similar analytical backgrounds who are looking to make their first move into industry. **Who should apply?** • ACA, ACCA or equivalent qualified accountant • Experience within Transaction Advisory, Deals Advisory, FP\&A or a similar analytical finance role • Strong financial modelling and analytical skills • Advanced Excel capability • Experience supporting budgeting, forecasting or business planning activities • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills • Financial services experience advantageous but not essential • Ambitious, commercially minded and keen to develop within a high\-performing team **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting into the Financial Analysis team, responsibilities will include: • Preparation of monthly financial dashboards and performance reporting • Analysis of revenue, expenses and key business metrics • Supporting budgeting and forecasting processes • Developing financial models and business analysis • Providing insight and recommendations to support decision\-making • Supporting business cases, investment analysis and strategic initiatives • Partnering with stakeholders across Finance and the wider business • Contributing to process improvement and reporting enhancements **Interested in this position?** To apply, please submit your CV to Emma, the consultant managing this assignment, via the link below.

title: Financial Analyst
description: A leading global law firm based in Dublin 2 is currently seeking to hire a Financial Analyst to join its high\-performing Finance team. Reporting directly to the Senior Finance Manager, this is an excellent opportunity for a newly or recently qualified ACA / ACCA accountant who is keen to develop their career within a dynamic, collaborative, and technology\-driven environment. This role offers strong exposure across the business and would particularly suit someone with an interest in financial analysis, reporting, systems, and process improvement. **Key Responsibilities** * Drive and maintain monthly reporting and KPI analysis across departments and individual fee earners * Deliver detailed financial analysis to support strategic decision\-making * Collaborate with the Finance Management team on reporting and presentations * Liaise with Partners and internal stakeholders on finance\-related queries * Support and mentor trainees where required * Contribute to finance projects, process improvements, and system enhancements * Assist with ad hoc reporting and analysis * Present KPI performance insights at fee earner level * Act as a key liaison between Finance and IT regarding the practice management system * Support software implementations and continuous improvement initiatives **Candidate Profile** * ACA / ACCA qualified with a strong academic background * 1–2 years’ PQE preferred, though newly qualified candidates will also be considered * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities * Excellent attention to detail and reconciliation skills * Advanced IT and Excel skills * Experience with finance systems or implementations is advantageous * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Proactive, organised, and collaborative approach **The Opportunity** * Excellent salary and benefits package * Hybrid working model * Genuine opportunities for career progression and internal development * Exposure to a highly regarded international professional services environment To apply or discuss this opportunity confidentially, please contact Richard Minchin at Lex Consultancy.

title: Financial Controller
description: Life changing therapies. Global impact. Bridge to thousands of biopharma companies and their patients. **We are PCI.** Our investment is in People who make an impact, drive progress and create a better tomorrow. Our strategy includes building teams across our global network to pioneer and shape the future of PCI. **Main Purpose Of Job** This role sits within our Site Finance Team and reports directly to the Finance Director. It is a hands\-on position that plays a key role in managing the day\-to\-day operations of the finance function. The successful candidate will also contribute to key projects and support the continued growth and development of the business. **Key Areas Of Responsibility Will Include** * Responsible for the preparation, analysis and reporting of the financial and operational performance of the site * Leading the day\-to\-day finance operations of the site, ensuring strong financial controls and governance * Manage and mentor a team across AP, AR, Treasury \& Reporting * Oversight of intercompany transactions and reconciliations * Owning the month\-end close process, ensuring accurate and timely reporting * Detailed variance analysis against budget and forecast * Management of all company taxes (VAT, PAYE, Corporation Tax) and ensuring compliance with Revenue rules * Providing clear financial insight and strategic support to the Finance Director and wider leadership team * Ensuring balance sheet integrity through robust reconciliations and review processes * Overseeing cash flow management and working capital optimisation * Maintaining and improving internal controls, policies, and financial procedures * Leading the annual group and statutory audit processes and acting as key contact for external auditors and tax advisors * Driving continuous improvement across systems, processes, and reporting * Developing and mentoring members of the Finance team **Qualifications, Experience \& Skills Required** * Qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) * 5\-10 years of experience, with at least 3\+ years in a similar position in a fast\-paced, multinational environment * Prior experience of managing a finance team (5\-10 employees) * Expert on intercompany accounting * Strong knowledge of accounting concepts (US GAAP, FRS102\) * Extensive experience of ERP systems (JD Edwards, Oracle) * Detailed focused, with excellent administrative and organisational skills * Strong IT skills, including proficiency in Excel, Word and PowerPoint * Strong commercial acumen and problem\-solving skills * Analytical with a strong attention to detail and the ability to work under pressure and to tight deadlines * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team with professionals at all levels and of various disciplines Join us and be part of building the bridge between life changing therapies and patients. We are committed to cultivating an inclusive workplace by holding ourselves accountable to the highest standards of understanding, fairness, respect, and equal opportunity – at every level. We envision a PCI community where everyone can belong and grow, and we strive to bring this vision to reality by continuously and intentionally assessing our people practices, policies and programs, marketing approach, and workplace culture.*

title: Financial Crimes Compliance Analyst
description: The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. The Role will report directly to the Head to Anti\-Financial Crime, Ireland and serves as a subject matter expert (SME) playing a critical role in safeguarding our European businesses against financial crime risks. Sitting within the second line Anti‑Financial Crime (AFC) function, this role carries significant responsibility in supporting Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services (FAS) business in the execution of the Financial Crime framework (which consists of AML and Terrorist financing, Sanctions, Bribery and Corruption and Tax Evasion) during the distribution of Vanguard UCITS range and oversight of Irish Fund's Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter\-Terrorist Financing (CFT) programme. **Core Responsibilities** * Support the maintenance and enhancement of the European AFC Framework, including methodology updates, documentation improvements, and alignment to group‑wide standards. * Advise the FAS business in respect to compliance with the Vanguard global financial crime policy requirements including local European obligations. * Create, update, and manage the Anti Financial Crime policies, procedures, and supplements. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute an assurance program for the areas that Vanguard Europe insources and support the creation of specific financial crime testing programs for oversight of outsourced third\-party relationships. * Review and analyse escalations in respect to due diligence, screening and transaction monitoring alerts. Resolves exceptions, including unusual and complex issues escalated by more junior team members, according to established guidelines. Collaborates with managers to resolve the most complex issues. * In collaboration with the UK AFC team execute of the Vanguard Europe annual risk assessments methodology for AML, Sanctions and Bribery and Corruption including detailed data gathering, quantitative and qualitative analysis, drafting of risk narratives, and coordination of validation and challenge with stakeholders across the First and Second line of defence. * Support the submission of periodic regulatory and internal AFC reporting for Vanguard Europe and the FAS business, ensuring all submissions are accurate, timely, complete, and delivered according to defined governance and regulatory expectations. * Assist project governance by helping prepare papers, progress updates, risk logs, and milestone reporting. * Provide formal and informal educational activities, including presentations, staff meetings and training programs. Develops and delivers financial crimes related training and education for Vanguard Europe crew members. * Maintains a comprehensive knowledge of the investment industry and compliance developments with implications for Vanguard's financial crimes program. Keeps abreast of the current market, regulatory and industry news. Serve as a consultant on compliance related issues. Acts as a liaison to regulatory agencies.Strong understanding of Anti Money Laundering/Counter Terrorist financing, sanctions and Anti Bribery and Corruption requirements, risk, regulatory expectations, industry practice, business systems and processes in Ireland and the UK. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Significant practical experience delivering or coordinating risk assessments, including data analysis, narrative writing, and validation stages. * Demonstrated experience managing regulatory or internal reporting cycles, including drafting, coordinating, and quality‑assuring submissions. * Experience working with policy governance, supporting policy refresh cycles, or maintaining standards frameworks. * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience drafting governance papers, risk assessments, or policy content. * High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and ability to work under pressure in a fast‑paced environment. * Ability to interpret regulatory expectations and apply them pragmatically. **Desirable** * Experience operating across multiple European jurisdictions and/or global AFC structures. * Experience of supporting the distribution of an Irish UCITS range. * Exposure to transformation or change programmes (e.g., technology enhancements, operating model changes). **Qualifications:** * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in AML/CFT, compliance, audit, or risk within financial services. Management Company/Asset Management 2nd Line AFC experience preferred. * Professional qualification relating to Financial Crime / Undergraduate degree or equivalent combination of training and experience **Special Factors:** * This position follows a hybrid working model that will require you to be in our Dublin office three days per week. * Vanguard is not offering visa sponsorship for this position. **How We Work** Vanguard has implemented a hybrid working model for the majority of our crew members, designed to capture the benefits of enhanced flexibility while enabling in\-person learning, collaboration, and connection. We believe our mission\-driven and highly collaborative culture is a critical enabler to support long\-term client outcomes and enrich the employee experience.

title: Financial Planning Advisor
description: **Fairstone Ireland** is seeking applications from experienced Financial Planning Advisors for full\-time permanent opportunities nationwide. As a Financial Planning Advisor, you’ll provide expert, independent financial advice to a diverse client base, helping them achieve their financial goals through tailored solutions. This position involves managing an established client portfolio while also focusing on growing new business opportunities. You will have significant autonomy and the chance to shape financial plans that drive measurable results. This role is part of the Fitness Probity Regime and Individual Accountability Framework, which set out the specific requirements applicable. **Responsibilities** * Deliver professional financial advice that complies with the Central Bank of Ireland’s regulations and Fairstone’s best practices. * Manage and grow an existing portfolio of clients, ensuring a proactive and client\-focused service. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to recommend financial solutions that address a wide range of client needs. * Drive new business growth through referrals, self\-generated leads, and existing Fairstone client relationships. * Ensure all client records are maintained accurately, documenting objectives, goals, and recommendations. * Stay ahead of industry trends, regulatory changes, and market developments to provide informed advice. * Actively participate in business development initiatives, contributing to the growth of client Assets Under Management (AUM) and overall revenue. **The ideal candidate** * A minimum of 12 months' experience as a Financial Planning Advisor, with a deep knowledge of financial planning, including protection, pensions, investments, tax\-efficient strategies, and holistic planning. * A proven track record of managing and growing client portfolios. * A high level of commercial awareness and the ability to identify opportunities for business growth. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build trust and explain complex information clearly and concisely. * Strong organisational skills, with a focus on accuracy and attention to detail. * A client\-centric approach, prioritising strong, long\-term relationships and exceptional service delivery. **Qualifications** * Fully QFA qualified is an absolute requirement. CFP is a distinct advantage. * In\-depth knowledge of financial regulations, markets, and industry practices. * Strong leadership qualities and the ability to mentor junior advisors, if required. * Proficient in using financial planning software and tools. Please note that any offer of employment with Fairstone Ireland is subject to the satisfactory completion of pre\-employment checks. These checks include verification of your references, education and professional qualifications, confirmation of eligibility to work in the Republic of Ireland, and a review of publicly available professional/personal information (internet search). Employment will not commence until all checks have been completed to our satisfaction. For more information, please email **hr@fairstone.ie** and the HR team will be able to assist you further.

title: Fluvial Geomorphologist - Hydromorphologist
description: **Overview** We are looking for a Hydromorphologist / Geomorphologist to join our team and work at the forefront of helping address the challenges of maintaining, creating or restoring water courses to/in good ecological status, ensuring that their form and function is incorporated into and/or protected from new infrastructure development, as well as restored and recovered from previous interventions that may have physically degraded them, e.g. channelisation, presence of weirs, culverts etc. We are undertaking an increasing number and range of important and exciting projects that involve assessing the current state of water courses and designing and developing methods and proposals for their restoration, maintenance or creation, dependent on the type of project. We are working for a range of clients such as water companies, community groups and other government and private sector entities and authorities. Building on this success, we are growing our team, and this growth could include you. You could help us to win and deliver work across our extensive client portfolio and enhance our reputation for excellence. As hydromorphologist, you will have experience of geomorphology or hydromorphology related project tasks in Ireland, the UK, Europe and/or internationally. You will work closely with our technical specialists who are based in our established teams across Ireland, both the Republic and in the North, and in the UK. We have a friendly and approachable team with a strong reputation for delivering excellence with multiple clients. Your role * With a degree in science, engineering or mathematics. * A post\-graduate qualification in hydromorphology or geomorphology, or a related subject, would be desirable. * You will have hands\-on, in\-the\-field, practical experience of hydromorphological assessment of river channels and water courses. * You will have experience as a hydromorphologist. * You will preferably understand fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology in relation to its role in determining Good Ecological Status/Potential under the Water Framework Directive. * Your experience will include geomorphology and hydromorphology, for example work on natural flood management, nature restoration, channel creation, maintenance and management. * You will have experience of delivering parts of, or whole, project tasks, such as data collection, data and information analysis, interpretation and drawing succinct conclusions and recommendations. * You will have good numerical, analytical, report\-writing and presentational skills and an eye for detail. * You will be highly motivated and proactive and enjoy solving challenging fluvial geomorphological problems within a high achieving team environment. * You will be able to communicate effectively with your colleagues to support collaborative working. * You will have valid full, clean, Irish driving license. **About You** * Demonstrable knowledge and practical use of one or more of the following would be an advantage: + River Hydromorphology Assessment Technique (RHAT) survey method for river water bodies. + The Lake Morphology and Modification Assessment System (Lake MImAS) for lakes. + Hydromorphological Quality Index (HQI) for transitional and coastal (TraC) waters, and/or Morphological Quality Index for Ireland (Quinlan and Mockler, 2020\). * Ability to survey, assess, appraise and report on the state of fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology in water courses and water bodies. * Knowledge and understanding of key fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology issues and their drivers, preferably in an Irish, UK or European context. * Experience of writing funding proposals, costing consultancy tenders and preparing technical and financial aspects of tenders is desirable. description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. We are looking for someone with analytical problem\-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate effectively within a team environment while communicating technical concepts clearly. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, implement, test, and monitor high\-quality software components for full stack applications * Develop and maintain backend services and APIs * * Experience with **SQL Server** and/or **PostgreSQL** * Understanding of **REST API design and integration** * Experience with **DevOps tools** (e.g. Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, or similar) * Experience supporting **application deployment and CI/CD processes** * Experience developing **scalable features and services** * Problem\-solving skills with a structured and thoughtful approach * Understanding of software development principles and best practices * Experience writing **unit tests** and participating in **code reviews** * Ability to collaborate within cross\-functional teams and communicate technical concepts clearly **Desirable Skills** * Knowledge of **SSIS** and ETL workflows, or interest in developing these skills * Experience with **data migration** activities or exposure to related tools and processes * Experience with **Power BI** and data platforms such as SQL Server or Oracle * Experience working with **AWS services** (e.g. S3, Lambda, Cognito, Amplify) * Experience working with **SSO and SAML** * Experience building products using **LLMs** * Basic prompt engineering knowledge or interest in learning * Interest in developing new systems and projects **Additional Information** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Salary Range: €45,000\-€60,500 * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Version 1 Pension with a 5% employee\-matched contribution, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. * 24 days holiday allowance plus Public Holidays, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& quarterly Star Awards. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. title: Full Stack Software Engineer, Charles River Development, Officer
description: ****Who We Are:**** Charles River Development (CRD) is the FinTech division of State Street, of which Charles River Private Market’s (CRPM) offers holistic solutions across clients public and private portfolios. Our vision is to be the world’s leading investment platform, shaping the future of the industry and driving new levels of technology\-led innovation, resiliency, efficiency and growth for investors and the people they serve. CRD Engineering organization is innovating and transforming the platform by moving away from monolithic software to utilising next generation cloud\-based technology that leverages Microsoft Azure, Kafka, Snowflake, etc. Industry momentum has seen CRD grow over 135% in headcount over the last 5 years, and we continue to grow. As a digital innovation leader, we invest into our solutions, processes, systems, and talent. We are actively hiring engineers for our Private Market’s division as this is an exciting time to join our innovative engineering teams as we are investing in growing out our European presence and leadership to help drive our next phase of growth. ****Summary:**** Act as Full Stack engineer responsible for the development, enhancement and maintenance of the CRPM modules and components, as part of an agile scrum team, and at times provide troubleshooting assistance to customer support teams. ****Responsibilities:**** * Analyze, design, develop, test, and debug large and complex software enhancements and solutions critical to system operation * Collaborate with engineering team to create simple and sustainable software solutions for complex problems * Direct problem solving for projects or major phases of projects to resolve software technical issues * Provide informed guidance and critical analysis of proposed changes during code reviews * Write unit and automation tests to ensure a high\-quality product * Provide troubleshooting on large, mission critical client implementations * Engage in the agile software development process by adhering to and advancing the CRD scrum methodology, including attending all daily standups, sprint planning, backlog grooming, and retrospectives * Plan and coordinate cross\-team activities to complete assignments ****Qualifications:**** **Recommended Education:** * B.S. degree (or foreign education equivalent) in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, and Physics or other technical course of study required. MS degree strongly preferred. ****Key Skills And Prior Experience:**** * Experience of 3\-5 years developing event driven architectures, financial services a plus * Experience in cloud native development using Java, Spring, Angular or React * Experience in developing observable, operable cloud native software that horizontally scales * Experience with relational databases (RDBMS) * Demonstrated problem solving abilities involving multi\-tiered, high\-performance database applications * Demonstrated ability to design and develop software solutions to business and technical problems of high complexity * Outstanding written and verbal communication skills * Able to work well with peers in a collaborative team environment * Knowledge of Agile development methodology * Employees are expected to work from the office 4 days a week. ****About State Street**** **What we do.** State Street is one of the largest custodian banks, asset managers and asset intelligence companies in the world. From technology to product innovation we're making our mark on the financial services industry. For more than two centuries, we've been helping our clients safeguard and steward the investments of millions of people. We provide investment servicing, data \& analytics, investments research \& trading and investment management to institutional clients. **Work, Live and Grow.** We make all efforts to create a great work environment. Our benefits packages are competitive and comprehensive. Details vary in locations, but you may expect generous medical care, insurance and savings plans among other perks. You'll have access to flexible Work Program to help match your needs. And our wealth of development programs and educational support will help you reach your full potential. **Inclusion, Diversity, and Social Responsibility.** We truly believe our employees' diverse backgrounds, experience and perspective are a powerful contributor to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive and reach their maximum potential while adding value to both our organization and our clients. We warmly welcome the candidates of diverse origin, background, ability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity and personality. Another fundamental value at State Street is active engagement with our communities around the world, both as a partner and a leader. You will have tools to help balance your professional and personal life, paid volunteer days, matching gift program and access to employee networks that help you stay connected to what matters to you. State Street is an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. Company: Charles River Development Discover more at www.StateStreet.com/careers ****About State Street**** Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success. We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work\-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law. Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers Read our CEO Statement Job ID: R\-783863

title: Fundraising Ambassador - MSF
description: **Looking for more than just a job?** Want to earn well, build confidence, and make a real difference every day? Join **Médecins Sans Frontières (Doctors Without Borders)** as a **Face‑to‑Face Fundraising Ambassador**, representing one of the world’s most trusted humanitarian organisations. You’ll be out in local neighbourhoods, having real conversations, sharing MSF’s life‑saving work, and inspiring people to support medical care where it’s needed most. **This role is NATIONWIDE:** **PLEASE NOTE:** A **full driving licence and access to your own vehicle are essential for this role.** No experience? **No problem.** Full training is provided from day one. **What you’ll be doing:** * Engaging with members of the public in residential areas * Representing MSF’s mission with passion and professionalism * Signing supporters up to long‑term, life‑saving donations * Working as part of a supportive, high‑energy outdoor team **Who this role is perfect for:** * Confident, friendly communicators who enjoy meeting people * Those happy working outdoors and on their feet * Reliable, motivated, and goal‑driven individuals * Caring, community‑minded people who want meaningful work * **Drivers with a full licence and access to a vehicle** **What you’ll get in return:** * **Base salary:** €2,166\.67 – €2,250\.00 **per month (gross)** * **Uncapped commission** – your effort directly increases your earnings * **Fully paid, industry‑leading training \& onboarding** with major Irish charities * Clear progression opportunities, including **leadership \& self‑development training** * **Opportunity to take Fridays off** when weekly targets are reached * Supportive management, coaching, and a strong team culture **Your Working Week:** * **Monday – Friday** * **Variable hours depending on site and campaign** * **Summer Shifts \- 11am \- 7pm OR 12pm \- 8pm** **1‑hour lunch break included**

title: Global Client Executive - Talent Solutions
description: LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We're also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that's built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed. Join us to transform the way the world works. **Job Description** This role is based Dublin , Ireland At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centered on trust and optimized for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team. As a LinkedIn Global Client Executive within the EMEA region, you will be the regional lead for our accounts responsible for both renewals and upselling on existing installation combined with a strong focus on developing consistent new business. You will partner closely with the Client Director on your accounts to expand our footprint by aligning LinkedIn’s portfolio of professional solutions (across Talent, Learning and WWU) with the complex requirements of our largest global customers. You will establish yourself as a peer and trusted advisor to senior level economic buyers at each of your clients and develop a deep understanding of their business and the markets in which they operate. You will also invest in and demonstrate an understanding of their strategy and how they execute on same. You will act as the single regional point of contact with our key global customers and conduct executive level commercial discussions with the intention of growing and capturing green, blue and white space. You will be responsible for being the lead on strategic clients with multiple POD language members for cohesion \& best outcome for the business. To ensure our clients generate the highest possible return on their investment, you will ensure that all LinkedIn Products and Solutions are being utilized effectively in the region in collaboration with cross\-functional partners and best practice. Ultimately, you will provide compelling recommendations for additional investments and renewals in LinkedIn. This is a highly collaborative role requiring a passion for building strong and lasting relationships both within LinkedIn and externally with our clients, supporting our largest customers to be, and feel, successful with LinkedIn products and solutions. You will need to lead and support a complex, cross\-functional team to ensure multi\-level engagement with the clients. **Responsibilities:** Success in this role will be your ability to excel in the following: **Client Focus** * Be open, collaborative with XFP and customer\-focused in all communications and transactions with colleagues and clients. * Leverage executive relationships and executive sponsors to deepen LinkedIn’s relationships with clients. * Quickly gain knowledge and expertise of the entire talent suite of LinkedIn Solutions (Talent, Learning \& Glint) to communicate the benefits of new products, features, and enhancements to our clients. * Provide high\-quality customer engagement activities on the solutions they are already invested in. **Sales Excellence** * Partner with the Global Client Director (GCD) of your named accounts to agree upon account goals, strategies and tactics for growth and then build the related account plans. * Own, build and drive the EMEA/LATAM level strategy for your accounts to both contribute to \& align with the global strategy. * Identify key decision makers, regional approach, buying processes, current investment, product utilization and new revenue opportunities. * Prospect to find new business through local entities with zero/low LinkedIn footprint by building weekly pipeline of net new business identification expectation of 1\.5\-2 new opportunities per week. * Negotiate and close all orders with the larger LinkedIn investment in mind and provide post\-contract support to ensure product delivery and satisfaction * Use Dynamics to track activities and, most importantly, accurately forecast your business having CRM always reflect best practice detail. **Leadership** * Coordinate and delegate a cross\-functional team of subject matter experts to support your accounts, drawing on product specialist support when necessary. * Solution sell to our clients to become a part of their tools \& technology ecosystem positioning LinkedIn as a key partner not a vendor. * Maintain ongoing communication with the GCD to keep them updated on new opportunities. * Represent both market trends and client need to the Executive and Product teams to ensure we are both serving current needs well and evolving our portfolio to identify future client needs. * Demonstrate exceptional deal management skills across deals of all levels. * Be active in driving diversity \& LinkedIn culture and Values within the team \& division. **Qualifications** **Basic Qualifications:** * 6\+ years of experience in quota\-carrying roles or equivalent sales consulting experience * 2\+ years in enterprise client's sales * Fluent in English **Preferred Qualifications:** * 1\+ years’ experience selling LinkedIn Learning. * Experience working cross\-functionally to build strategy in EMEA to enhance global deal success. * Success selling to senior talent, learning executives and business partners on the solution of LinkedIn * Proven success in selling brand new, disruptive technology * Client success mindset to maintain legacy revenues and protect long term revenue opportunities. * Experience closing new business accounts and managing existing accounts * Excellent communication and teamwork skills * Demonstrated ability to generate a plan to ensure deep penetration into global accounts and regional entities **Suggested Skills:** * Global Sales * Field/Territory Strategy * Collaboration * Negotiation * Forecasting **Additional Information** **Global Data Privacy Notice and Compliance Posters for Job Candidates** Please use this link to access documents that provide information about how LinkedIn handles the personal data of employees and job applicants, as well as the E\-Verify Participation Notice and the Department of Justice Immigrant and Employee Rights Section Right to Work posters: https://www.linkedin.com/legal/candidate\-portal.

title: Graduate Engineer
description: **Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?** Sony Interactive Entertainment isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we’re part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting\-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity. **The Role** We are looking for a motivated Engineer to join our team and help create and improve tools in the PlayStation SDK used by game developers worldwide These include tools to manage content, perform firmware updates, load and run game code, carry out remote configuration, capture console, screen and controller logs, remote view consoles and also a distributed resource management system. We are offering a 12\-month fixed\-term contract as part of our wider Graduate Programme within the Product and Technology group. This programme is designed to provide a structured early\-career experience, combining hands\-on work with dedicated development opportunities. As part of the scheme, you will have access to a speaker series featuring leaders and experts from across the organisation, a range of training opportunities to build professional skills, and regular opportunities to connect and network with fellow graduates across the Product and Technology organisation **What You’ll Be Doing** * Analysis of customer requirements to produce feature and or application designs * Provide estimates, design, develop and deliver work to schedule. * Investigate, debug, and resolve issues with both existing and new code features * Participate in requirement and architectural discussions, collaborate with other teams and work in an agile environment. * Attend periodic in\-person workshops or team events (with the possibility of travel) **What We Are Looking For** This is an entry level opportunity. You should bring some of the following skills and experience: * BSc /MSc in Computer Science or equivalent experience * 1\-2 years experience programming in a high level language with a preference towards C\# or C\+\+ * Ability to demonstrate understanding of algorithms, data structures and software architecture * Strong debugging, profiling and performance tuning skills **Highly Desirable Skills** * Experience with UI frameworks and technologies including .NET, Avalonia, WPF and / or MFC * Skilled in using Git, TeamCity, or similar CI/CD workflows * Knowledge of dynamic scripting languages (e.g.Python) Please note, Sony Interactive Entertainment conducts background checks at the offer stage for all new employees (which may include criminal background checks for some roles) and will need to process personal information to support these checks. Please refer to our Candidate Privacy Notice for more information about what personal information we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and your data protection rights. **Equal Opportunity Statement:** *Sony is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All persons will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender (including gender identity, gender expression and gender reassignment), race (including colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin), religion or belief, marital or civil partnership status, disability, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, trade union membership or membership in any other legally protected category.* *We strive to create an inclusive environment, empower employees and embrace diversity. We encourage everyone to respond.* *Sony Interactive Entertainment is a Fair Chance employer and qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records will be considered for employment.*

title: Graduate Health and Safety Consultant - 2026 Graduate Programme
description: The Opportunity A career at Arup offers you the chance to make a positive difference in the world. Independently owned and independently minded, Arup is the creative force at the heart of many of the world's most prominent projects in the built environment. When you join Arup, you immediately become part of our global community working on some of the world's most recognised projects. Dedicated to sustainable development, Arup is a collective of advisors and experts working across 140 countries. Founded to be both humane and excellent, we collaborate with our clients and partners using imagination, technology and rigour to shape a better world. As a Graduate Health and Safety Consultant at Arup, you will: * Carrying out the duties of Project Supervisor Design Process (PSDP) co\-ordinator on projects of varying sizes and complexities * Carrying out design health and safety reviews with our design teams on projects * Provision of advice to clients and designers in relation to construction, operational and occupational health and safety * Providing assistance to the Company Safety Officers in the implementation of our occupational safety management systems (which is OHSAS 18001 accredited) * Carrying out design and construction safety reviews/inspections on construction sites and operational facilities Does this role inspire you? We're looking for graduates who are currently pursuing a third level qualification in Health and Safety. **We Also Value** * Third level qualification in another technical area within the construction industry, e.g. Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management or similar * Some knowledge of design and construction\-related health and safety legislation * Some understanding of the PSDP role * Some knowledge of Safety Management Systems * Excellent communications, inter\-personal and writing skills * Pro\-active approach to gathering and processing information with good follow\-through to completion * Some construction site experience will also be an advantage Please submit a a copy of your CV, Cover letter and most recent transcripts when applying.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Danish Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English * Fluency in Danish * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Group Financial Controller
description: **Group Financial Controller** **Location:** On\-site, 5 days per week \- North Dublin **Reports to:** Chief Financial Officer An exciting opportunity has arisen to join a PE\-backed construction group experiencing significant growth. As Group Financial Controller, you will be a key member of the senior finance team, driving financial reporting, controls, and continuous improvement across the organisation. You will lead a large finance team, working closely with the CFO and Group Cost Accountant to support strategic decision\-making and business development. **Responsibilities** * **Financial Reporting** — Lead the preparation and analysis of Group Financial Statements to strict deadlines, including trading updates and commentary papers for stakeholders. * **Forecasting** — Take ownership of financial forecasts for the group and its subsidiary companies. * **Team Leadership** — Manage and develop large accounting teams, providing guidance on technical accounting matters and ensuring ongoing training and professional development. * **Audit \& Compliance** — Oversee the annual external audit engagement, ensuring timely completion and that all statutory requirements are met. * **Financial Controls** — Ensure robust financial controls are in place and consistently applied across the group. * **Continuous Improvement** — Identify and lead projects to enhance finance processes, leveraging technology and systems to improve efficiency, customer service, and the control environment. * **Stakeholder Engagement** — Liaise with key internal and external stakeholders, providing support to business development activities as required. **Requirements** **Essential** * Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) * Minimum 10 years' post\-qualification experience in a senior finance role, ideally within the construction sector * Strong technical accounting expertise * Proven ability to lead and develop teams in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment * Experience managing external audit relationships * Excellent attention to detail and ability to meet tight deadlines **Desirable** * Strong commercial acumen with a track record on commercial projects * Good working knowledge of Irish tax legislation * Experience in complex, multi\-entity group structures * Project management and delivery experience **Personal Attributes** * Excellent communication, influencing, and negotiation skills * Ability to build positive relationships at all levels * Initiative, problem\-solving ability, and a strong record of achievement **Remuneration** * Up to €140,000 plus bonus \& benefits **Contact** * Rachel Tubridy on 086 662 9674 or email rtubridy@peoplesource.ie ***PeopleSource is a finance and accounting recruitment firm.This role will not qualify for sponsorship; therefore all applicants must have the right to work in Ireland.*** ***Due to the level of responses for recently posted roles, similar is expected for this advert also, only successful applicants will be contacted. Apologies in advance but the volume of applications has been too high to be able to contact everyone with feedback. Thanks for understanding.***

title: HR Business Partner
description: Turner \& Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries. Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success. Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions. **Job Description** The HR Business Partner for the Infrastructure team in Ireland is a strategic partner to the leadership team, driving initiatives aligned to the Europe and Country HR strategies as well as the local businesses long\-term business objectives. The HR Business Partner will be a key advisor in building organizational capability, developing future leaders, and ensuring the company’s HR practices support sustainable growth across the Infrastructure sector. This role requires an individual who is able to assure compliance with employment law, while creating a positive and high performance\-driven culture. The ideal candidate is someone who thrives in a fast\-paced and complex environment, can navigate complex employment challenges, and be the trusted adviser to the leadership team. **Responsibilities:** * Act as trusted partner of the senior leadership team to understand business objectives, growth plans, anticipate workforce needs and identify critical talent gaps. * Act as the subject matter expert and proactively manage all employee relations issues and HR related risk mitigation. * Work closely with the leadership team and implement succession strategies for key roles that align with long\-term growth plans. * Serve as a trusted advisor on organizational development, change management, and leadership alignment. * Drive leadership development initiatives to strengthen the pipeline of future leaders. * Drive management development initiatives to strengthen the calibre of managers within the business. * Be proficient in the use of HR Management Information and enable the business to use HRMI to make informed, data\-led decisions on key investment and development opportunities and drive performance outcomes. * Work with Talent Acquisition to launch and support Early Career programmes such as apprenticeships, Summer Placements and Graduate schemes. * Work with Global Mobility and local team to ensure the visa/sponsorship process is seamlessly managed for employees and clients. * Adapt the Europe HR strategy to local country needs, legal requirements, goals; implement it and track key performance indicators which align with organization goals and initiatives. * Implement a culture of continuous improvement, resulting in identification of opportunities for process improvement and efficiency in coordination with Europe HR. * Attend management meetings to represent the HR function, presenting HR\-related data, engaging in discussions, and understanding strategic implications of HR policies and practices. Present required information for your requests and proposals, allowing the Leadership team to make informed decisions. * Drive global initiatives for performance management, salary and promotions review and others. * Ensure 100% data accuracy for all HR\-systems. * Promote successful and timely implementation of the new HRIS. * Remain up to date on wider industry and HR best practice ensuring that Turner and Townsend are able to attract and retain the best talent. * Support the annual Infrastructure conference, ensuring HR related information is prepared and shared with wider business. * All other duties as assigned. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources Management, Business Management, or similar. * 5\+ years of experience in a Business Partner role with increasing responsibility in a complex and global environment. * Leadership abilities and people management experience. * Solid knowledge of local labor regulations in Ireland. * Hands\-on experience with payroll preparation and collaboration with external suppliers. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Ability to summarize and present complex HR topics and issues concisely and clearly, both verbally and visually, tailored to the target audience. * Ability to manage and influence in a cross\-functional matrix environment. * Assertiveness but also willingness to compromise. * Strong negotiation skills. * Solid project management skills to drive initiatives and processes. * Discretion in dealing with highly confidential and sensitive business and people related information exhibited at all times. **Additional Information** *Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change.* *We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance.* *Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.* *Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/* Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn *It is strictly against Turner \& Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner \& Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.* *Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner \& Townsend personal e\-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner \& Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorised Recruitment Agency/Search Firm for Turner \& Townsend, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by the Recruitment Team, to submit candidates for review.*

title: HR Service Partners - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The EIB, the European Union's bank is seeking to recruit for its Human Resources Directorate (HR) – HR Business Partnering Division (HRBP) \- HR Service Partner Unit (HRSP), at its headquarters in Luxembourg, **HR Service Partners\*** . * Benchmark role Senior/Principal Operational Assistant. ***These are full time temporary positions at grade 2/3\.*** **The Terms Of These Contracts Will Be** ***Until 31/12/2027 for the first position*** ***Until 31/12/2028 for the second position*** ***Panel interviews are anticipated for July/August 2026*** Purpose Provide operational and administrative support to HR Business Partnering activities in order to deliver an optimal HR service to managers and colleagues across the Bank, ensuring the right level of data confidentiality. He/She interfaces with other HR services and with managers and staff members of the client directorates. The post requires flexibility, autonomy and the ability to prioritise and handle several tasks simultaneously, while exercising a high attention to detail. It also requires a high level of discretion due to the confidential nature of the matters under the remit of HR Department. Peoplesoft/Oracle Cloud HCM , ensuring accuracy and confidentiality. * Perform regular data checks and contribute to reliable reporting for decision\-making. * Provide analysis of available HR reports for the designated Directorates to the HRBPs or directly to managers to support the decision\-making process on HR matters, providing feedback to the relevant teams when adjustments or corrections are needed. * Execute other regular or ad\-hoc HR operational and administrative tasks for the covered Directorate(s) in alignment with the respective HRBP(s). * Initiate improvements and participate in the optimisation of working methods, processes and technologies and raise new ideas that ultimately foster customer satisfaction and efficiency. * When needed, ensure integration and on\-the\-job training of more junior colleagues. * Maintain permanent relationships and good cooperation with Expert HR teams (Talent Acquisition, Performance, Learning \& Development, Talent Management, Customer Service, etc.) to guarantee an optimal flow of information within HR. * Build and maintain good working relationships with the clients in the business, peers and internal counterparts. **Qualifications** * Secondary level education, complemented with a 2\-year certification in a relevant field or secondary level education with equally qualified experience. * At least 5 years of experience in an administrative support role or operational position, with at least 2 years of experience in HR administration with a solid knowledge of HR processes. * Strong collaboration and organinsation skills, pro\-active attitude, solution and service oriented. * Developed sense of discretion. * Ability to multi\-task, prioritize, and manage time effectively. * Good knowledge of the standard office tools and particularly advanced skills in Excel. * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage). Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* *level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. title: Human Resources Coordinator
description: **Job Opportunity: HR Coordinator** Are you an ambitious HR professional looking to elevate your career within a high\-performing, fast\-paced environment? We are exclusively partnering with a highly successful, Irish\-headquartered global leader to find a dynamic **HR Coordinator** to join their evolving team. This is a brilliant opportunity to play a key role in an organization dedicated to best\-practice HR and continuous growth. **The Role at a Glance:** * **Location:** North Dublin (Free onsite parking) * **Working Model:** 50/50 Hybrid working model * **Reporting to:** HR Director **Key Responsibilities:** As a core member of the HR team, you will provide comprehensive support across all aspects of the employee lifecycle. Key focus areas include: * **HR Systems:** Act as a key member of the HRIS implementation team, ensuring data accuracy and generating insight reports. * **Talent \& Onboarding:** Partner with hiring managers on recruitment and lead a seamless onboarding/induction process for new joiners. * **Payroll \& Benefits Collaboration:** Partner closely with the payroll team to maintain employee records for weekly/monthly processing and assist with pension administration. * **Employee Relations \& Queries:** Act as the first point of contact for HR queries, advising on company policies, and supporting employee relations matters (disciplinary/grievance processes, investigations, and note\-taking). * **Culture \& Engagement:** Support employee engagement initiatives, coordinate with the EAP provider, and help promote a positive workplace culture. **What We Are Looking For:** * **Experience:** Minimum 2\+ years of HR experience in a fast\-paced environment (FMCG industry experience is highly desirable). * **Education:** Minimum Level 8 third\-level qualification, ideally in HR or a business\-related discipline. * **Tech\-Savvy:** Solid experience working with HRIS systems (e.g., CoreHR, Workday, or TMS). Experience with system implementation is a major plus! * **Knowledge:** Excellent understanding of Irish employment legislation and experience assisting with ER cases. * **Attributes:** A proactive, resilient professional comfortable navigating periods of change and balancing multiple priorities. * **Driving license** : An Irish driving license is required for this role. **To Apply:** If you are ready to take the next step in your HR journey with a premier global business, click Apply!

title: Human Resources Generalist
description: **Job Description** Guggenheim Securities International Limited – London **About The Position** Guggenheim Securities International Limited is seeking an experienced Human Resources Generalist to join our HR team supporting operations in the UK and Ireland. Based in our London office, this position will support a growing office of approximately 60 employees and be the local point of contact for HR processes which are centralized in the U.S. In partnership with U.S. Recruitment, Talent and Development, and HR Operations, this position will provide local support for global programs for all HR products, services and new initiatives. **Essential Job Functions** * Onboarding new hires to ensure a “best in class” experience * Facilitate a seamless enrollment process for payroll, benefits, pension, etc., using Workday and other platforms * Coordinate new hire meet and greets for senior hires * Complete Right to Work Checks on or before employee start date for all new hires * Obtain Regulatory References for new hires where applicable and respond to requests for Regulatory References for former employees * Conduct New Hire Orientation sessions * Ensure desk space and technology is up and running on day one Ongoing Support * Provide expertise on Firm’s HR processes, policies, and programs * Conduct periodic check\-in meetings with employees throughout their employment * Assist U.S. Recruitment with full\-time and intern recruitment coordination * Serve as an extension of the U.S. talent team to assist in performance management, talent review and promotion processes * Escalate employee concerns and assist as needed with employee relations issues * Support the HR team with administrative activities and special projects as needed Off boarding * Conduct Exit Interviews with departing employees. * Complete all required offboarding steps coordinating with relevant parties/departments **Preferred Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources or a related field * 7\+ years of Human Resources experience in financial services, preferably in investment banking * 2 – 3 years of Workday experience; ability to enter and audit WD data for the UK and Ireland * Strong interpersonal and communications skills, with the ability to present relevant facts needed to drive HR decisions * Strong attention to detail, well organized, excellent follow\-up * Grace under pressure, a strong customer service orientation but able to manage expectations appropriately * Ability to operate successfully in a fast\-paced environment handling multiple assignments at the same time * Must be a team player and able to work across multiple functions, disciplines and geographies and possess strong collaboration and communication skills Work Location * Currently, this role is expected to be in the London office at least 4 days per week. **About Us** Guggenheim Securities is the investment banking and capital markets business of Guggenheim Partners, a global investment and advisory firm. Guggenheim Securities offers services that fall into four broad categories: Advisory, Financing, Sales and Trading, and Research. Guggenheim Securities is headquartered in New York, with additional offices in Chicago, Boston, Atlanta, San Francisco, and Houston. For more information, please visit GuggenheimSecurities.com, follow us on LinkedIn or contact us at GSLateralRecruiting@guggenheimpartners.com or 212\.518\.9200\. **Guggenheim Securities, LLC (“GS”) does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications. GS considers any resume or application to be unsolicited if (a) received from an entity or individual without a current recruiting agreement with GS or (b) submitted to anyone at the firm other than through the process set forth in the recruiting agreement between GS and the submitting entity or individual, and GS will not pay a fee to any entity or individual for such submission.**

title: IT Analyst - Business Solutions
description: We’re looking for an IT Analyst \- Business Solutions on a 12\-month secondment to join our IT Solution Delivery Team, helping to bridge the gap between business needs and technology solutions. This role plays a key part in translating business requirements into effective IT designs and supporting the delivery of technology solutions that drive business value. Based in Dublin, you’ll work in a collaborative environment, partnering with business stakeholders, IT teams, and external vendors. This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to impactful IT projects, with a primary focus on digital marketing, while gaining exposure across functions such as Sales, HR, and Supply Chain, and helping shape the future of technology within the organisation. **A Taste of What You’ll Be Doing** * **Business Requirements Gathering –** Lead analysis and documentation of business requirements and translate them into clear functional specifications for IT solutions and ERP systems. * **Solution Design \& Delivery** – Support the design, development, and implementation of technology solutions, ensuring alignment with business needs. * **Stakeholder Collaboration** – Build strong relationships with business partners and IT teams to ensure solutions align with business strategy. * **Project Planning \& Estimation** – Contribute to project planning and develop time and cost estimates based on requirements and resource needs. * **Testing \& Implementation Support** – Assist with test case development and support SIT/UAT processes to ensure successful solution delivery. **We’re Looking for Someone With** * Proven experience in an IT Analyst or Business Analyst role * Strong experience working closely with business functions (e.g. Digital Marketing, Sales, HR, Supply Chain) * Demonstrated ability to gather, analyse, and document business requirements * Solid understanding of IT processes and solution delivery lifecycle * Experience contributing to the successful delivery of IT projects * Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and prioritise competing demands * Proactive problem\-solving mindset with the ability to work independently **What’s Next** After you apply, your application will be reviewed by a real recruiter, so it may take us a few weeks to get back with you by email or phone. If you join our team, you’ll be rewarded for the difference you make. Our comprehensive benefits offer you the support you need through your life events, big or small. Need assistance throughout the application or hiring process? Email **European.recruitment@kellanova.com** **Get to Know Us** We’ve always been driven to unleash the full potential of our differentiated brands and our passionate people. Our iconic, world\-class brands at Kellanova include Pringles, Cheez\-It, Pop\-Tarts, MorningStar Farms, Special K, Krave, Zucaritas, Tresor, Crunchy Nut, among others. Mars, Incorporated has acquired Kellanova in a transaction that brings together iconic brands, storied legacies and outstanding capabilities with a view to shape the future of snacking. You can learn more here, and our hiring teams will be happy to discuss further questions if your application advances in the hiring process. Let’s shape the future of snacking. Kellanova is an Equal Opportunity Employer that strives to provide an inclusive work environment, a seat for everyone at the table, and embraces the diverse talent of its people. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ethnicity, disability, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, sexual orientation, age, protected veteran status

title: IT Helpdesk Technician
description: **Department:** IT **Location:** Dublin **Description** BDO Ireland are seeking a skilled IT Technician to join our growing team, providing exceptional technical support both onsite and remotely. The ideal candidate will have a passion for delivering professional and friendly assistance to end\-users while effectively managing and resolving IT incidents. This role requires a meticulous approach to logging, analysing, and prioritizing issues, ensuring timely resolution or escalation when necessary. If you have a strong background in laptop support, application installations, Active Directory tasks, and Office 365 administration, along with a knack for providing top\-notch user support across various platforms and devices, we want to hear from you. **Key Responsibilities** * Providing IT support in a professional and friendly manner both onsite and remotely. * Accurately log, analyse, prioritise, and solve (or escalate if required) I.T. incidents, always ensuring that the ticket is updated on the IT Helpdesk ticketing system. * Assist end users in the resolution of incidents using both remote\-control tools and desk\-side support. * Hardware support \& maintenance (e.g. Reimaging of computers) * Software installations \& support * Active Directory Task * Office 365 Setup \& Administration * General user support for office applications, O365, Windows 10, iPhone / iPad, and Corporate Mobile device/Smart Phone Support. * Support and management of multi\-function devices. * Provide IT Support for in house meetings \& events with audio visual equipment. **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** * Minimum of 1 year experience in a similar role. * Excellent interpersonal skills with a proven ability to communicate on various levels with peers, customers, and external parties. * A good working knowledge of Microsoft Office 365\. * Experience in supporting Windows 10 \& 11 in a corporate environment. * Strong desire to learn and improve skills and knowledge. * Proven troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills. * Familiarity with remote\-control tools and desk\-side support techniques. * Relevant certifications (e.g., CompTIA A\+, Microsoft Certified IT Professional) are a plus. * Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Excellent team player with a team\-oriented mindset. **Benefits** * Generous annual leave, with flexibility to purchase additional days. * Coverage of relevant professional subscriptions. * Access to a contributory pension scheme. * Health and wellbeing services. * Career development opportunities. * Flexible remote working options. * Family\-friendly leave policies. * Complimentary coffee and onsite gym. * Modern office in a central Dublin location.
