title: IT Support Analyst
description: ITSUP014819\_1 **We’re hiring for an IT Support Analyst to support our growing Technology team!** To keep up with our growth in the UK and Ireland, we’re looking for a dedicated **IT Support Analyst** to join our growing Technology team. You will act as the first point of contact for employees seeking technical assistance for technology issues, providing technical support over the phone, in\-person, or other means of communications while maintaining high levels of customer service. **We’re Looking For Someone With** * 2\+ years of experience working for a fast\-paced corporate level Service Desk * Good knowledge of Windows Operating System (Win10 / 11\) * Good knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (2013, 2016, O365\) * Basic understanding of networking * Familiarity with mobile devices (smartphones/tablets) * Clean driving license * Excellent communication skills with ability to problem solve, quickly identify and adapt to end users * Technical abilities while maintaining the highest levels of customer service * Critical thinking in a fast\-paced environment with ability to be flexible * Highly dependable with exceptional teamwork mentality **Using These Skills, You’ll Be** * Providing technical support and hardware management across Ireland based offices * Providing support for all Technology products and services * Assisting with management of mobile devices for NFP Application usage * Executing all technical onboarding and offboarding duties * Testing, documenting, and assisting with corporate initiatives as assigned by Manager * Following standard Support Technician operating procedures; accurately logging all * Supporting Technician requests using NFP ticketing system, ServiceNow **Who is NFP?** With Over 600 People In The UK And Ireland And Over 8,000 Employees Worldwide, NFP Is Part Of The Aon Group And Specialise In Helping Businesses In Four Core Areas * Insurance (helping them manage key risks) * Health and safety (supporting them to create a safer workplace for their employees) * Employee benefits (helping them reward their people more effectively) * HR, people and talent (supporting employers and their people to thrive through changes and challenges) **What You’ll Love About Working Here** * working in a dynamic, fast\-paced organisation in an exciting industry * the opportunity to do globally impactful work from day one * learning from industry and business line specialists with decades of experience * a huge variety of projects to work on and challenges to solve * our People First culture, which illustrates our commitment to your wellbeing and development, not just as an employee but as a human being * a rich suite of employee benefits and out\-of\-work perk **The Great Benefits We Offer** **Finances** It’s important to know you’re paid fairly for the hard work you put in, which is why we complete regular reviews to keep your salary in line as you progress. title: Interoperability Specialist
description: **Interoperability Specialist** The Mater Private Network, a leader in Irish private healthcare, is seeking a Interoperability Specialist This is a permanent, full\-time position. **Purpose of the role** We are seeking an Interoperability Specialist to design, build, support, and continuously improve healthcare data exchange across our IT organisation. The role will focus on API\-led integration, HL7 and FHIR\-based interoperability, and the delivery of secure, reliable interfaces using InterSystems Health Connect Cloud. This is a hands\-on technical role requiring strong healthcare integration experience, advanced SQL skills, and programming expertise. The successful candidate will work with clinical, operational, vendor, architecture, security, and IT teams to ensure that healthcare data flows accurately, safely, and efficiently between internal systems, cloud platforms, and external partners. **Skills \& Qualifications** Essential * Strong experience in healthcare IT integration or interoperability roles. * Hands\-on experience with HL7 v2 and FHIR\-based integration. * Practical experience designing, consuming, or exposing APIs, especially REST APIs. * Experience with InterSystems Health Connect, HealthShare, Ensemble, IRIS for Health, or preferably InterSystems Health Connect Cloud. * Strong SQL capability, including querying, joins, data profiling, troubleshooting, reconciliation, and performance\-aware data analysis. * Programming or scripting experience in at least one modern language, with the ability to build tools, automate tasks, and solve technical integration problems. * Strong understanding of healthcare workflows and clinical/administrative data flows. * Experience with JSON, XML, message transformation, data mapping, validation, and interface testing. * Ability to investigate complex integration issues across applications, databases, APIs, middleware, and external systems. * Strong documentation skills, including interface specifications, mapping documents, test evidence, and support runbooks. * Ability to work effectively with technical teams, clinical stakeholders, vendors, and governance groups. Desirable Experience * Direct experience with InterSystems Health Connect Cloud. * Experience with InterSystems ObjectScript, productions, business services, business processes, business operations, DTLs, and message tracing. * Experience with FHIR servers, terminology services, implementation guides, SMART on FHIR, OAuth2/OIDC, or API gateways. * Knowledge of healthcare coding and terminology standards such as SNOMED CT, LOINC, ICD, dm\+d, or local equivalents. * Experience with cloud\-hosted integration platforms and modern DevOps practices. * Familiarity with CI/CD, source control, automated testing, environment management, and infrastructure\-as\-code concepts. * Experience with data privacy, GDPR, cybersecurity, audit, and healthcare information governance. * Experience supporting national or regional health data exchange programmes. * Qualifications * Degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Information Systems, Health Informatics, Software Engineering, Data Engineering, or a related discipline * Relevant certifications in HL7/FHIR, InterSystems technologies, cloud platforms, software development, date, or health informatics would be advantageous Job description available on request. MATER PRIVATE NETWORK IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER

title: Investment Corporate Actions Representative III/Associate Analyst
description: **Job Description** **Corporate Actions Analyst – Income \& Class Actions.** **Location: Dublin** Perform daily tasks associated with the processing and reconciliation of voluntary and mandatory corporate actions, as well as research Class Action cases. Process corporate actions promptly and correctly with policies, procedures, and regulations. Interact with various groups across the organization as well as external vendors and business partners. The **Expertise** We’re Looking For * 1\-2 years of relevant Financial Services experience. * Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions processing experience preferable. * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience. The **Purpose** of Your Role * Conduct and keep track of corporate action activities. * Conduct processing of all types of corporate action events, both voluntary and mandatory. * Facilitate the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions and provide daily cash estimates. * Coordinate corporate action executions, ensure the collection of income to the funds, resolve position differences, and facilitate securities lending activities. * Assist the team with corporate action research and process tasks related to assets under management. * Collaborate with stakeholders on issues and continuously follow up to resolve them. * Escalate high\-risk items or seek assistance to mitigate loss. * Develop the knowledge required to grasp product and processing rules while working under the direction of more experienced colleagues. * Display a strong sense of teamwork. * Explore and communicate new ways of improving existing processes. The **Skills** You Bring * Your knowledge of the key elements of Corporate Actions processing * Your knowledge of the Custody industry, Fund Accounting and Mutual Fund Operations * Your ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * Your ability to identify and escalate high risk items * Your strong written and verbal communications skills * Your time management skills * Your analytical and problem\-solving skills * Your Innovative mindset – you look for new ways to improve current processes and develop creative solutions. The **Value** You Deliver * Responsibility for high quality processing of Mandatory and Voluntary corporate actions * Managing the communication of complex corporate actions to internal stakeholders * Interacting with many groups within the Fidelity organization and effectively representing the business to customers and business partners * Escalating issues appropriately * Demonstrating a strong team\-work ethic How Your Work **Impacts** the Organization The Global Middle Office acts as a servicing centre for FMR Co.'s trading desks globally, facilitating the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions with brokers and custodian banks, and providing daily cash estimates to Trading. It is also responsible for coordinating corporate action executions, ensuring the collection of income to the Funds, resolving position differences and facilitating securities lending activities. **Company Overview** At Fidelity, we are focused on making our financial expertise broadly accessible and effective in helping people live the lives they want. We are a privately held company that places a high degree of value in creating and nurturing a work environment that attracts the best talent and reflects our commitment to our associates. Fidelity established an Ireland presence in 1996 and we currently employ 1000 staff across IT and shared services, with offices in Dublin (City West) and Galway (Parkmore East). We are currently operating under a hybrid model, with some flexibility for remote working. However, Fidelity is gradually transitioning towards a full\-time return to the office, as we believe this will help strengthen our culture and enhance both the associate and customer experience. At present, this transition is expected to begin from 2027 onwards, although no fixed timeline has been confirmed. For information about working at Fidelity, visit www.fidelityinvestments.ie. Fidelity Investments is an equal opportunity employer. **Category** Investment Operations

title: Junior Data Operations Analyst
description: We are bringing on a Junior Data Operations Analyst to support our current engagement with an international financial services partner. Much of this work involves the heavy lifting of data: making sure information flows correctly between their existing internal systems and the new tools we are building for them. You will be helping us keep their databases clean and their automated reports running accurately. This is a full\-time, fully remote position where you will focus on the practical details of data management and integrity. **About Pixel Systems** We are a small, remote software agency that builds the tools teams actually use. We do not spend our time on flashy marketing sites or the latest tech fads. Instead, we focus on the practical stuff: admin panels, CRM integrations, and the database migrations that people usually try to avoid. Our clients come to us because we are straightforward. We provide fixed quotes, show our work every week through demos, and hand over everything (the code, the documentation, and walkthrough videos) when we are finished. We use reliable, well\-tested technologies like Node, React, and Postgres because we want the software we build to be stable and easy to maintain. **The role** As a Junior Data Operations Analyst, your day is mostly about the data itself. You will be working with our developers to map out how information should move from one system to another. Sometimes that means writing scripts to clean up messy spreadsheets so they can be imported into a database without errors. Other times, it means checking that an API integration is correctly pulling financial records. You will spend a lot of time looking at database tables, identifying inconsistencies, and fixing them. It is not high\-profile work, but it is the work that ensures the systems we build are actually useful for our financial services partner. Since we are a remote shop, you will spend your time in our task management tools and document everything you do so the rest of the team stays in the loop. **What we are looking for** We need someone who is organized and enjoys the process of getting data in order. Since this is an entry\-level role, you do not need years of professional experience, but you should be the kind of person who double\-checks their own work and pays attention to small details (like whether a date format is consistent across a file). You should be comfortable working on your own while staying connected with our team through written communication. Being able to explain why a data error happened in plain English is more important to us than knowing every complex database query. We value people who are happy to dig into a boring spreadsheet to find a broken link because they know it matters for the final product. **Helpful background** While we will help you get up to speed, it is useful if you have a bit of experience with a few basic tools. These are not strict requirements, but they will help you get started: * A general understanding of how databases work, specifically SQL\-based systems like Postgres. * Experience using GitHub to manage projects or track changes in your work. * Basic knowledge of how APIs work to move data between different software programs. * Some familiarity with JavaScript or Node, as these are the primary tools we use. * The ability to use Excel or Google Sheets for more than just basic lists. * A preference for simple, working solutions over complex ones. **Working at Pixel Systems** Working here is different from a typical corporate job. We are a small team without layers of middle management. You will work directly with our senior developers and see how software projects are handled from start to finish. We value work that is finished and functional. Because we are a fully remote team, we rely heavily on clear writing and regular check\-ins. The position is paid, and we offer a salary that is competitive for the role and the current market. There are no frantic all\-nighters or sudden changes in direction here. We plan our work, we stick to the schedule, and we ship tools that solve real operational headaches for our partners. We are more interested in your ability to learn and your attention to detail than a long list of previous jobs. We build software that supports the day\-to\-day operations of businesses, and that requires a steady hand and a focus on accuracy. If you like the idea of working in a small, quiet, and deliberate environment where the goal is to build things that work, you will fit in well here. We focus on getting the job done right so that our clients can stop worrying about their data and get back to their own work.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Job Description** At IPS, we are global leaders in developing innovative solutions for the consulting, architecture, engineering, project controls, procurement, construction management, and CQV of technically complex facilities, primarily in the life sciences industry. We are looking for a talented **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our growing construction team to be based at our EMEA office located in **Dublin, Ireland** . We are looking to appoint a construction industry professional with expert knowledge of construction costs and contracts. In this role, you will be responsible for managing all aspects of the contractual and financial side of construction projects and ensuring that construction projects are completed within their projected budget. The Quantity Surveyor assists with the valuation of construction works, bidding and project budgeting, and the submission of bills to the client, in addition to estimating services to projects and business development efforts. **Job Duties And Responsibilities** * Prepares service estimates for projects as required to support the achievement of corporate objectives * Prepares constructed cost estimates for projects as required to support achievement of corporate objectives * Supports the design, implementation, and maintenance of estimating programs and systems for the corporation * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to coordinate the acquisition of input information for the estimating process for projects * Prepares project estimates using drawings, specifications, scope, basis of design, and other project information as input * Maintains files of project estimates and supporting backup documentation for reference during project execution * Supports the evaluation of historical data, including purchase orders, subcontracts, force reports, and productivity reports, and incorporates relevant data into the company unit cost, material, and labor factors * Monitors subcontract and purchase order awards, compares awarded costs to budgeted costs, determines the bases for variances, and adjusts estimating systems, models, and unit pricing to adjust future estimates * Reviews design options and recommends the best solutions based on cost, engineering quality, or availability of materials * Maintains relationships in the industry to facilitate the acquisition of estimating data and processes * Prepares estimates to support the change management process for projects * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to monitor project implementation for compliance with the basis of the project estimate as required * Evaluates required estimate contents by reviewing requests for proposal, attending pre\-bid meetings, and communicating with proposal team to support preparation of bids and proposals during bid/proposal preparation phase * Special projects as assigned **Qualifications \& Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline, or an equivalent technical degree. * Significant experience in Project Delivery, including Estimating and Cost. * 3\-6\+ years relevant industry experience. * Exposure to MEP and Cost Estimating systems is a distinct advantage. **Skills** * Written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to write clear reports to convey complex information simply to a diverse range of people. * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build and develop relationships. * Numerical and data analysis skills. * A creative and innovative approach to problem\-solving. * IT skills and the ability to learn sophisticated design and costing IT packages. * Teamwork skills and the ability to motivate and lead those on\-site. * Project management skills. * Resilience, determination, and the ability to work well under pressure. * Good commercial awareness. * A practical, logical, and methodical approach to work. * Attention to detail. * Detailed knowledge of past and current building and construction technology, processes, materials, business, and legal matters. All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with **video required** . **About Us** **IPS** , a Berkshire Hathaway company, is a global leader in technical consulting, architecture, engineering, procurement, construction management, commissioning, qualification, and validation services for technically complex facilities. **Integration** is how we think and work. It requires the ability to unite existing and emerging technologies into a process that reliably delivers products and accelerates speed to market. That end\-to\-end mindset allows us to be agile and anticipate challenges as we move projects toward operational readiness. We infuse our curiosity, our passion, and our commitment to partnership in delivering solutions at any scale. We meet our clients where they are and get them to where they need to go. title: Junior Security Engineer
description: Larian Studios is looking for a motivated and hands\-on Junior Security Engineer to help strengthen and support the security posture of our global studio environment. This role is ideal for someone early in their cybersecurity career who wants exposure to infrastructure security, vulnerability management, monitoring, and operational security within the gaming industry. You will work closely with IT, infrastructure, and development teams to support security operations, investigate threats, improve security controls, and help maintain best practices across the organisation. ### **Responsibilities** * Assist in identifying, assessing, and mitigating vulnerabilities across infrastructure and applications * Support the deployment and operation of security tools across multiple systems and environments * Perform security monitoring, vulnerability scanning, and assist with log analysis to detect incidents and threats * Support technical security audits and help provide actionable recommendations * Assist in maintaining secure baseline configurations for Windows and Linux systems * Participate in incident handling and escalation procedures * Help educate staff on information security best practices * Research and learn new technologies, tools, and security techniques * Maintain up\-to\-date security documentation and operational procedures * Participate in change management workflows to support secure implementations ### **Requirements** * 1–3 years of experience in IT, infrastructure, or cybersecurity\-related roles * Basic understanding of cybersecurity principles and industry best practices * Familiarity with vulnerability scanning or security monitoring tools * Basic understanding of Windows and Linux operating systems * Basic exposure to scripting or automation tools such as PowerShell, Bash, or Python * Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills * Good written and verbal communication skills in English * Passion for cybersecurity and willingness to learn * Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment ### **Nice to Haves** * Exposure to SIEM, IDS/IPS, endpoint protection, or vulnerability management tools * Interest in learning security automation and scripting * Experience supporting multi\-site or international environments * Cloud platform exposure (AWS, Azure, or GCP) * Interest in gaming or gaming industry environments * Understanding of networking fundamentals ### **What we offer** * An international team of over 500 employees spread across 7 studios around the world, each of which have their own unique qualities, and maintain a small\-studio feel * An open, collaborative environment where ideas are welcome and encouraged * A managerial team consisting of highly experienced game developers at the top of their fields * Sharing hobbies and passions with like\-minded colleagues * Inclusive environment with many activities organized by the studios or the employees * Commitment to making the best game possible for the fans, including listening to community feedback

title: Junior Software Engineer, Elixir
description: **About Telnyx** *Telnyx is an industry leader that's not just imagining the future of global connectivity—we're building it. From architecting and amplifying the reach of a* *private, global, multi\-cloud IP network* *, to bringing* *hyperlocal edge* *technology right to your fingertips through intuitive APIs, we're shaping a new era of seamless interconnection between people, devices, and applications.* *We're driven by a desire to transform and modernize what's antiquated, automate the manual, and solve real\-world problems through innovative connectivity solutions. As a testament to our success, we're proud to stand as a financially stable and profitable company. Our robust profitability allows us not only to invest in pioneering technologies but also to foster an environment of continuous learning and growth for our team.* *Our collective vision is a world where borderless connectivity fuels limitless innovation. By joining us, you can be part of laying the foundations for this interconnected future. We're currently seeking passionate individuals who are excited about the opportunity to contribute to an industry\-shaping company while growing their own skills and careers.* **About You** We’re looking for people with a strong background or interest in building successful products or systems; you’re comfortable in dealing with lots of moving pieces; you have exquisite attention to detail; and you’re comfortable learning new technologies and systems. **The Opportunity** The team you’ll be joining is tasked with developing and maintaining the software that enables seamless and efficient delivery of Telnyx's communication and connectivity services. While you’ll be responsible for the stack that allows customers to interact with our products and platform (think api\-gateway, authentication, rate\-limiting, etc), you’ll also work with other engineering squads to continuously improve the way they expose and monitor their services. * Design, build and maintain APIs, services, and systems across Telnyx’s engineering teams. * Debug production issues across gateway, auth, network, and application layers. * Work with engineers across the company to build new features at large\-scale, both internally and externally. * Improve engineering standards, tooling, processes, observability and reliability **What We're Looking For** * Have 1\-2 years of professional software development experience with an object\-oriented or functional programming language (Elixir is preferred). * Have an appreciation of functional programming and a strong desire to become an expert Elixir programmer. * You take a thoughtful approach to decision making, knowing when to move fast and when to long\-term optimize. * Have experience working with relational databases at scale. * Understand the challenges inherent in concurrent and distributed programming. * Have deep knowledge of microservices architecture patterns and anti\-patterns. * Have familiarity with Docker, Kubernetes, Consul, Prometheus, Grafana and/or RabbitMQ. * You keep up to date with the latest technologies and patterns, and enjoy sharing what you’ve learned. * You're a team player and willing to put the team's success ahead of your own. **Bonus Points For** * Experience building or operating customer\-facing APIs, gateways, auth systems, or internal platforms. * You want to work in a fast\-paced, product\-driven environment. * Have experience with Envoy

title: Junior Sous Chef (Full time) (Glasthule/Dun Laoghaire based)
description: \*\* Must be within commutable distance of Sandycove/Glasthule to be considered for this postition \*\* At Cavistons Seafood Restaurant we are currently recruiting for a Junior Sous Chef to join our established kitchen team. We work daily to provide our customers with excellent dishes and hospitality whilst dining in our newest edition, our restaurant. We specialise in predominantly fresh seafood and meat dishes, with a focus on local, organic and wild Irish produce as much as possible.. The candidate must have the following: \- 3 Years experience in kitchen management at Sous Chef level \- Experienced in preparation of all aspects of meat and fresh fish produce \- Experience in A la carte cooking and service, private functions and banqueting. \- Be passionate about delivering high quality modern dishes with frequent changing menus. \- Have the ability to work within a team, mentoring staff and guiding junior staff. \- Capable of leading the kitchen team, ensuring guidelines are achieved daily. \- Assist head chef in menu creating and food development. \- Full HACCP training In return we offer: \- A balanced working week with shared early and late shifts amongst management. \- An environment that will allow you to express your qualities in cooking with fantastic produce. \- An opportunity to grow and learn from many other aspects of the overall business, allowing you to express your skills within. \- Employee discounts across the company. Duties: Main daily duties will include: \- Leading a team for morning/ evening service, ensuring quantities required are prepared \& individual sections meet required time demands. \- Assist head chef in stock control and daily orders. \- Help to progress and train members of staff in a positive but successful manner. \- Health and Safety \- Ensuring the kitchen hygiene standards remain high throughout the working day, regular HACCP checks. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €39,000\.00\-€43,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme Application question(s): * Please outline your previous relevant experience for this role? Language: * English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Junior Systems Administrator
description: **Junior Systems Administrator** **Location: Dundalk** **About Us** GenetiQ delivers perfect\-fit software solutions that elevate the businesses of our customers. Powered by our customer\-focused, collaborative and innovative team, our software is used by more than 2,500 businesses worldwide. Continuously innovating since 1992, our open and trusting environment has created lasting relationships. GenetiQ's founders and many of our first team members are still a core part of the organisation. Our employees drive our culture and success and our goal is to inspire, support and help them to develop – and enjoy – their careers. GenetiQ was honoured in the Deloitte Fast 50 for the fourth year running in 2020\. To stay future\-focused, we are always looking for dynamic team players who are customer\-focused and share our enthusiasm for technology. If this sounds like you, then come elevate your career at GenetiQ. **Scope of Role** The rewarding career you are looking for is at GenetiQ Software. If you enjoy working with technology and thrive in a hands\-on, fast\-paced environment, this role offers an excellent opportunity to build foundational IT skills while supporting core business systems. As a Junior Systems Administrator within the Internal Systems Team, you will support the day\-to\-day operation, maintenance, and security of GenetiQ’s IT environment. You will gain exposure to a wide range of technologies including Microsoft 365, Windows devices, user access management, and core infrastructure. Working under the guidance of senior Internal Systems staff, you will contribute to the delivery of reliable and secure IT services across the organisation, while developing the technical and professional capabilities required to progress within the Internal Systems function. **Responsibilities** The Junior Systems Administrator will support the Internal Systems Team in delivering high\-quality IT services across the business. Your responsibilities will include assisting with user support, system administration tasks, and maintaining operational stability across core systems. Key areas of responsibility include: * Supporting user onboarding, offboarding, and access changes (joiners, movers, leavers) * Managing user accounts, permissions, and groups within Active Directory / Entra ID * Assisting with Microsoft 365 administration including mailboxes, licences, and access controls * Providing end\-user support for laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and peripherals * Supporting device setup, provisioning, and configuration (e.g. Intune / Autopilot) * Troubleshooting basic hardware, software, and connectivity issues, escalating where required * Assisting with the maintenance of Windows servers, cloud services, and business systems * Performing routine system checks, updates, and maintenance activities * Supporting backup, monitoring, and basic security processes under supervision * Following IT security policies and reporting potential risks or incidents * Maintaining accurate IT documentation, asset registers, and user guides * Contributing to continuous improvement of IT processes and standards **Skills and Abilities** A person that has some of the following skills and abilities would be at a distinct advantage: * A logical and structured approach to problem\-solving with strong attention to detail * Ability to troubleshoot technical issues and identify appropriate solutions * Willingness to learn and develop new technical skills * Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a team * Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts in a clear and user\-friendly way * Good organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * A professional, reliable, and customer\-focused approach **Preferred Qualifications and Experience** A person applying for this role must have: * A qualification in IT, Computer Science, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience * 1 year \+ experience in an IT support, helpdesk, or technical environment * Experience in any of the following is desirable but not essential, as training will be provided: * Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Microsoft 365 administration (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint) * Device management tools such as Intune * Basic networking concepts (DNS, DHCP, IP addressing) * Windows 10/11 environments and basic Windows Server exposure * IT ticketing or ITSM systems * Awareness of IT security best practices Relevant certifications (or working towards) are advantageous, for example: * Microsoft Fundamentals (MS\-900, AZ\-900\) * CompTIA A\+ or Network\+ **Benefits and Culture** Our perfect fit ethos extends to our employees, ensuring that our roles, environment, benefits and progression paths are geared toward their professional and personal development. As noted, our employees drive our culture. Our iSocial group organises a number of GenetiQ social events and activities throughout the year, such as summer BBQs, golf outings, charity hikes and more. In addition to a competitive salary and a robust social calendar, we offer a great range of benefits including: * **Healthcare:** Your health and well\-being matter to us. GenetiQ offers private healthcare to its employees. * **Flexibility:** Our flexible working plan helps employees to manage a better work\-life balance. * **Pension Plan:** GenetiQ contributes a % of an employees salary, helping them plan for the future. * **Exercise Reimbursement:** Whether it's a gym or a golf club membership, we support the efforts of employees to keep active. * **Holidays:** GenetiQ offers 25 days of paid leave, plus statutory bank holidays.

title: KONE Apprentice program, Lift and escalator service
description: **Start your engineering career with a global leader** . At KONE, we don’t just offer apprenticeship — we build careers. Our Apprentice Programme 2026 gives you the opportunity to gain hands\-on experience, earn industry‑recognised qualification and learn from experts who help to shape the future of cities. **Why choose KONE Apprentice program?** * Earn while you learn with paid, work‑based training * Learn from experienced engineers on live sites * Work with a global leader that moves 2 billion people every day * Build a career in a growing, future‑focused industry **What you'll do as an Apprentice** * Hands\-on experience: Shadow and work with experienced Engineers on various sites. * Service and maintenance of lifts and escalators: Get involved in service visits and minor repairs of the equipment. * Customer interaction: Deal efficiently and effectively with customers. * Study: 1\-2 days a week is spent at the College, studying for nationally\-recognised qualifications in the same profession as the apprenticeship. **Practical information** * Working Week: Monday \- Thursday, 8:30am \- 5:00pm. On Friday from 8:30am – 4:30pm. * Hours of work: 39 per week. * Duration: 36 months (24 months the College course, 12\+ months NVQ3\) * Start date: 1st of September 2026\. * Salary: starting at EUR 10 per hour with annual increases subject to performance. **Location** We offer apprenticeship in **Dublin.** Training is **work‑based** , primarily at customer sites, so you must be prepared to travel. Travel to and from customer sites takes place outside standard working hours. **Who can apply?** * Age: Applicants should be aged 18 or over at the start of the apprenticeship (1st September 2026\), reflecting the level of prior educational or hands\-on experience required (typically around two years). * Education: qualifications in Maths and English. If you need to improve your GCSE grades, the College offers Essential Skills courses to support you. * Skills \& qualities we’re looking for: practical and hands\-on approach, motivation and interest to engineering, logical thinking, good time management and organisation, customer service, good communication. Positive attitude, persistence, and proactive approach. * The role is field‑based, so you will be expected to obtain a driving licence within the first 1–2 years of the apprenticeship. **Equal Opportunities** KONE is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all backgrounds (gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual preference, etc.). Proud partners of **Women Into Construction** . *At KONE, we are focused on creating an innovative and collaborative working culture where we value the contribution of each individual. Employee engagement is a key focus area for us and we encourage participation and the sharing of information and ideas. Sustainability is an integral part of our culture and the daily practice. We follow ethical business practices and we seek to develop a culture of working together where co\-workers trust and respect each other and good performance is recognized. In being a great place to work, we are proud to offer a range of experiences and opportunities that will help you to achieve your career and personal goals and enable you to live a healthy and balanced life.* Read more on *https://careers.kone.com/en/*

title: Kitchen Assistant/General Operative
description: **Beechfield Care Group** are currently seeking to recruit a general operative to assume responsibility for the care of residents at Beechfield Manor, Shanganagh Rd, Shankill, Dublin D18 P389 We are a long\-established Nursing Home Group based Dublin, Carlow, Meath, Westmeath \& Kildare. Our mission is to provide the best standard of quality care to our residents. Our philosophy places the care of the resident at the heart of the nursing home. **About Beechfield Manor** Our home is yours and, set within large period garden spaces, we provide unrivalled facilities close to Dublin's coastline. But what really makes us special is our residents, families, and staff. Every resident is unique, and we respect and value each individual for who they are. This is our ethos. We really have the most amazing people living and working here that are truly dedicated to their work **bout the Role:** The Housekeeper/general operative will be responsible for overseeing housekeeping cleaning operations at our nursing home. The working hours will be varied according to the home needs and will require occasional weekend work. **Role Responsibilities:** O This role requires the holder to work across the nursing home as a v Housekeeping Assistant v Catering Assistant v Laundry Assistant O The role requires the holder to be continuously vigilant towards the dignity, comfort, and safety of residents in the nursing home. O You are also required to continuously adhere to all hygiene, health and safety regulations. **Role Requirements:** O Excellent people skills with the ability to lead and motivate a diverse team. O Ability to identify areas for improvement and implement changes. O Strong organisation and planning skills to ensure that staffing is adequate across the home \& supplies are kept in order and replaced on units as required. O Team layer and able to work with management and staff. O Visible and credible leader who can lead by example. **Great benefits on offer:** O Free Meals on site O Paid training O Free parking O Flexible working hours O Education \& Development O Summer \& Christmas Parties O Access to Pension Scheme O Employee Assistance Programme O Long service, Employee recognition and appreciation awards

title: Kitchen Porter
description: We are hiring a Kitchen Porter to join our team in our latest venue in Kildare Village This is a fantastic opportunity for someone who enjoys the fast\-paced environment of working in a bustling restaurant. **Reasons to join our team:** * Career Growth \& Fast Progression * A Social, Energetic, PeopleFirst Environment * Variety, Creativity \& No Two Days the Same * Stability, Training \& Professional Development * Pride, Purpose \& Team Experience **The ideal candidate for Kitchen Porter position will have:** * Excellent communication skills * Attention to detail * Some Haccp knowledge * A desire to provide a consistently high standard of customer service * The ability to multi\-task in a fast paced kitchen environment * A good command of the English Language * Impeccable self\-presentation * To carry out cash handling procedures in accordance with Company policy * Previous experience as Wait staff is an advantage **What We Offer** * Fun staff days out * % off our sister sites * Superb training and progression opportunities * Gratuities shared * Competitive salary * Free Staff Meals on Duty * Employee Assistance Program * Employee Recognition Awards Apply with your CV today in the strictest of confidence.

title: Kitchen Porters Full Time Flexible
description: **Kitchen Support– 4\* Grand Hotel** Under the FBD Hotels \& Resorts management, an excellent opportunity has arisen to join our team for Kitchen Porters at the Grand Hotel, Malahide. Previous experience in a similar role would be preferrable. **Candidates should only apply if they can work fully flexible hours and get to and from Malahide without relying on public transport as shifts will be outside of Train/Bus hours.** **Objective:** To assist the Culinary Team in maintaining the standards in areas such as hygiene and food safety. The successful candidate will work closely with our Head Chef in the busy kitchen of the Grand Hotel. **Responsibilities will include:** * Maintaining a high level of operational hygiene in all Kitchen areas in compliance with Hotel and Health and Safety regulations * Carrying out cleaning duties as outlined in the cleaning schedule and complete daily / weekly cleaning checklists for all cleaning tasks performed * Cleaning pots, pans and general Kitchen equipment as required, ensuring correct storage * Thorough deep cleaning of Kitchen as required including equipment and storage areas * Dealing with stock rotation and deliveries **What are we looking for?** We are seeking an enthusiastic, motivated and hardworking individual with a strong commitment to quality, high standards, service and customer care. **Perks of joining the team:** We have a very friendly and helpful team and we hope you will fit right into our family. We provide: * Great Remuneration package * Meals on duty * Complementary Staff parking * Complementary use of the Arena Fitness Club * Staff recognition \& awards * Family and Friends discounted rates across F\&B Hotels Group * Taxsaver Scheme * Bike to work Scheme * Cash saving scheme * 'Refer a friend' scheme

title: Landscape Horticulturist (Portrane)
description: **What you'll do...** Work as part of a team looking after a specific purpose landscape. This will involve maintaining areas of lawn, planting, trees and surrounding hard and soft landscape elements. **What you'll need** * To have previous experience in landscape maintenance, be physically fit, hard\-working, be able to work alone and as part of a team, with a positive attitude, the ability to demonstrate initiative, be able to get to and from the site, and have a good eye for detail. **These would be good too...** * Machine and mower experience * A horticultural qualification * A full clean driving licence * Safe pass and manual handling certification **Who should apply?** * Professionals already in the industry and seeking to develop their careers * Horticulture graduates and students * Students seeking seasonal work * Candidates meeting the above criteria and available for immediate start in the Portrane area. Candidates can also apply from other areas but as part of our environmental commitment, we are trying to reduce transport requirements. **What are the career opportunities?** This position is targetted at developing someone to work as a foreperson, As Ireland's oldest and most respected full\-service landscape company we can offer career advancement in areas such as design, construction, sports, maintenance, environment, sales, administration and quantity surveying. We pride ourselves on fostering new talent and helping career\-focused individuals to achieve their goals. Pay: €13\.00\-€18\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Portrane, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Have you previously worked as a landscape maintenance foreperson? Experience: * Landscape maintenance: 2 years (required) Licence/Certification: * Safepass Card and manual handling certificate (required) Work Location: In person

title: Lawyer - Projects and Construction
description: **Title: Senior Associate** **Department: Projects and Construction** William Fry is seeking a dedicated and ambitious senior lawyer to join our dynamic Projects \& Construction team at our Dublin office. This team provides expert advice on a wide range of construction, energy, infrastructure, and procurement matters. In this role, you will engage in a variety of complex transactions across diverse sectors including commercial development, residential projects, infrastructure, transport, and energy. Additionally, you will collaborate closely with other departments, such as Real Estate, Banking, and Corporate, on construction\-related aspects of transactions, disputes, development projects, financing, refinancing, and M\&A transactions. This is an exciting opportunity to join a high\-performing, and supportive team and work with a broad client base while contributing to the firm's ongoing success. **The Role** In this role you will be responsible for * Providing advice on a variety of construction\-related legal issues, including project procurement, disputes, project management, mid\-stream issues like extensions of time (EOT) and claims, negotiation of construction contracts, and agreements related to project financing and development, as well as construction elements in M\&A transactions. * Drafting and negotiating key contracts and agreements for diverse project types. * Participating in some of Ireland's largest and most complex construction projects, engaging with large Irish and global clients, including developers, contractors, and funders. **The Person** * You have a minimum of 5 years post\-qualification experience (PQE) within a reputable law firm or in\-house legal team, with a focus on construction, energy infrastructure, and/or other major projects. * You are proficient in drafting and negotiating construction contracts (especially RIAI, NEC, and professional appointments) and related documents, with a solid understanding of market requirements. * You possess strong interpersonal skills and have the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * You are client focused and have a proven track record of building and maintaining strong client relationships. * You are interested in mentoring and developing junior lawyers on the team. **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, San Francisco, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 460 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. title: Logistics Administrator
description: Do you enjoy working with data, systems, and people to keep operations running smoothly? Are you organised, detail‑focused, and confident managing administrative and financial tasks in a fast‑paced environment? If so, you could be the newest addition to our team as a Logistics Administrator. **Be part of the movement – and play your part.** **About The Role** Working Monday to Friday, 08:30 \- 17:00, this role sits at the centre of a busy logistics operation, providing administrative and financial support to ensure bookings, rates, invoicing, and documentation are handled accurately and on time. You’ll be confident working with systems and data, with excellent attention to detail, particularly when handling numbers, rates, and financial information. Strong IT skills, especially in Excel, are essential. You’ll thrive in this role if you’re organised, proactive, and able to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑moving environment. You’ll be a strong communicator, comfortable liaising with customers, suppliers, and internal teams, and have a natural ability to investigate and resolve issues. A positive attitude, reliability, and a strong sense of ownership will set you up for success. We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join. **Working at DFDS** Join the movement and grow with us. Be a valued member of a diverse team at DFDS and help drive change together. While you’re with us, we want you to develop. At DFDS, we believe that development happens first and foremost through activities in the workplace. We will also offer training courses that can support you in your role, if needed. Now, let’s talk about what we can offer you. **Benefits** When you join us, you become part of a fantastic business that genuinely invests in the growth and development of its people. Alongside a supportive culture, you’ll have access to a range of excellent benefits, including: * Pension Scheme – After successfully completing your probation, you’ll have the opportunity to join our Company Pension Scheme. Plus, members of the scheme may also be eligible for life assurance (subject to criteria). * Holiday Purchase Scheme – Flexibility to buy additional holiday days to suit your lifestyle. * Bike to Work Scheme – Save money and stay active with our cycle\-to\-work initiative. * HSF Assist – Our employee assistance programme offers a variety of support services, including a GP Advice Line, Virtual Doctor, Counselling and Emotional Wellbeing Support, and a Legal Helpline. * Health Plan with HSF – Enjoy benefits like cashback on dental treatments, glasses, and more. * MyGym – Access great discounts at hundreds of gyms, health clubs, leisure centres, outdoor bootcamps, and studios offering yoga, Pilates, CrossFit, and more. * Free Ferry Travel – Enjoy unlimited free ferry travel for up to four guests between Dover and Calais or Newcastle and Amsterdam. **Are we a match? Then what are you waiting for? Apply Now.** If you think your personality, skills, experience, and desire match this role, we’d love to hear from you. Please send us your CV as soon as possible, as we’re keen to meet potential candidates on an ongoing basis. **We look forward to hearing from you.**

title: Mid-Market AI Business Partner - Spanish speaking
description: We are seeking an intellectually curious Mid\-Market AI Business Partner (Spanish speaking) to work directly with Mid\-Market customers. This role is focused on engaging senior business leaders (often FTSE 250 \& Fortune 500 companies) in **high\-value, consultative conversations around AI transformation, data strategy, and business value creation** . This is not a traditional SDR or sales development role. Instead, it is a **strategic discovery and advisory position** , responsible for shaping early\-stage executive dialogue, influencing account strategy, and identifying where AI can unlock meaningful business outcomes. The role focuses on quality over volume, driving well‑researched consultative conversations, identifying the problems end customers are trying to resolve with AI and the use cases \& value the business are hoping to unlock. You will own the early to mid\-sales cycle; from targeted prospecting, account mapping, deep account research through to qualification and warm handoffs. Acting as the first impression and the face of the brand to executive buyers (up to c\-level), you will demonstrate strong executive presence, clear storytelling, and a deep understanding of AI and SaaS solutions to help identify opportunities for the end customer. Success in this role comes from a curious mindset to perform deep discovery, technical competency, \& a passion in AI and its application within a business. **Key Responsibilities** - Prospect and qualify new business opportunities through thoughtful, targeted outreach. - Lead executive level discovery conversations (c\-suite/c\-1\). Engage C‑suite and senior stakeholders with clear, confident, and consultative communication. - Shape account strategy, not just pipeline. This includes mapping complex organisations and stakeholders - Act as a thought partner to mid\-market customers through driving deep, structured discovery to uncover root business problems, challenge assumptions and connect technical capability with business value. - Partner closely with Account Directors on account planning \& development, research, and meeting preparation. Identify opportunities and build strategic hypotheses on where AI can deliver value - Own qualification and handoff, ensuring Account Directors receive well‑defined, high‑quality opportunities. - Maintain accurate CRM data, forecasting, and activity records. - Continuously build AI \& market expertise. Stay current on AI, SaaS, and market trends to communicate complex concepts clearly. **Qualifications** - Native or near‑native English \& Spanish with exceptional communication skills. - Minimum 1\-2 years' B2B sales experience required - 3\+ years’ experience in tech sales, inside sales, or business development (AI/SaaS/enterprise preferred). - A strong background/experience with Account Planning and Development - Strong technical competency, curiosity and ability to simplify complex topics. Background in AI, data, SaaS or digital transformation preferred - Exceptional storytelling \& communication skills - Curiosity \& problem solving to take structured approaches to discovery \& hypothesis building - Professional presence to face into executive levels - Detail‑oriented, research‑driven, and experienced with Salesforce or similar CRM tools. **Why Join** You’ll join a high‑bar sales team that values precision, credibility, and strategic impact. This role suits sales professionals who take pride in how they show up, operate with integrity, and bring discipline and insight to every executive conversation.

We empower millions to digitally connect with care providers, essential health resources and needed treatments – and enable care providers, employers, health plans and life sciences companies to meaningfully enhance quality, outcomes and value. We are dedicated to helping our partners evolve and modernize to meet emerging patient and marketplace needs. Fuze Health’s foundation is built upon the strategic combination of several proven, technology\-powered innovators in the digital health, diagnostics, and pharmacy sectors. Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next\-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. **MuleSoft Integration Engineer \- Fuze Health** Join our team to take ownership of our MuleSoft platform and drive our integration strategy forward. As a MuleSoft Integration Engineer, you will design, develop, and maintain secure, scalable APIs, Mule applications and integration flows using the Anypoint Platform. You will play a critical role in centralizing our integrations across Fuze Health to deliver a unified API\-led architecture that supports our growth and AI readiness. **Key Responsibilities** * Design \& Development: Design and build reusable, purposeful APIs and apps following API\-led connectivity principles. * Maintenance \& Support: Oversee daily operations and health of the MuleSoft platform, including troubleshooting production issues and managing security and network issues. Platform Governance: Implement and manage security policies, access controls, and governance standards within the Anypoint Platform. * Collaboration: Work closely with Salesforce developers, Architects, and business stakeholders to translate requirements into technical solutions. * Optimization: Perform performance tuning, monitor infrastructure resources, and optimize data throughput and latency. * Testing: Develop maintainable test automation and ensure high code coverage for all integration artifacts. **Requirements** - Experience: Minimum 3–5 years of hands\-on experience with MuleSoft Anypoint Platform (Mule 4, Anypoint Studio, CloudHub 2\.0\). - Technical Skills: * Proficiency in DataWeave for complex data transformations. * Strong knowledge of REST/SOAP APIs, JSON, XML, and RAML/OpenAPI specifications. * Experience with CI/CD tools (Git, Jenkins, Maven) and DevOps practices. Familiarity with security protocols like HTTPS, TLS, OAuth2, and certificate management. * Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field. * Certifications: MuleSoft Certified Developer or Platform Architect preferred. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience integrating with Salesforce, AWS, SAP, Snowflake. * Knowledge of event\-driven architectures (Kafka, AsyncAPI). * Exposure to MuleSoft RPA, Composer, or Intelligent Document Processing (IDP). Understanding of Agentic AI and its integration with CRM ecosystems. **Benefits** Alongside a base salary range of €66,500 to €83,000 we offer a range of benefits including: * Health insurance and an Employee Assistance Programme * Pension * Annual Compensation Reviews * Free monthly LetsGetChecked tests as we are not only focused on the well being of our patients but also the well being of our teams * A referral bonus programme to reward you for helping us hire the best talent * Internal Opportunities and Careers Clinics to help you progress your career within the company * Maternity, Paternity, Parental and Wedding leave * LetsGetChecked has a flexible annual leave policy At Fuze Health, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Fuze Corporate Inc. and its subsidiaries respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information. title: Network Development Engineer , European Sovereign Cloud
description: **Description** Would you like to be an Engineer that builds the Cloud, rather than an Engineer that just uses it? At Amazon Web Services (AWS) our Network Development Engineers look after the behind\-the\-scenes network and tools that make the world's largest cloud computing infrastructure possible. As a Network Development Engineer you will be responsible for building and operating the network infrastructure where you will learn the tools, services and components that the team relies upon every day. Through close collaboration, support and exchange of ideas, we share a common goal to serve our external customers, with seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own and deliver them. If you are motivated and passionate about the work that you do, care about delivering for your customers, have a love for learning and working with new technologies, then we welcome you to join us. You will be working with the most advanced tools, processes and talented people in the industry to make the Cloud a reality. Key job responsibilities **Skills** A Network Development Engineer is responsible for engineering, implementation, scalability, operations, reliability, and sustainability of our next\-generation networks. An NDE will utilise their networking skills along with our advanced tools and systems to invent, refine and develop automation and mechanisms to reduce manual operations. Our Engineers think at “Amazon scale” to solve problems in permanent, sustainable, and scalable ways, with an eye towards utilising the best solution over the quickest solution. The work will include: * Leading network projects in the areas of network sustaining engineering, network deployment/implementation, network scaling, technology refresh, best practices application, and/or network optimization * Influencing our operations function, responding to customer impact events and requests, troubleshooting, and fixing and improving the automation that runs our network * Analysing the network using tools and metrics to ensure it is operating correctly, safely and securely * Investigating technical issues scientifically and thoroughly, and assist in fixing complex problems so they don't come back * Successfully planning, delivering and simplifying new systems, processes, and tools to better deliver for our customers * Delivering sustainable and repeatable solutions and processes, optimising and removing bottlenecks * Understanding new and upcoming networking systems and developing ways of safely implementing them into our environment A day in the life An NDE is responsible for ensuring all network fabrics and devices are operating as expected. An NDE will utilise their networking skills along with available tools and services to troubleshoot and resolve any issues that have not been remediated through automation and to identify the root cause. They are expected to invent, refine and develop new automation and tools to reduce manual operations and fulfil the requirements of the business and customers. **About The Team** The Network Development team drives engineering, implementation, scalability, operations, reliability, and sustainability for all of the network fabrics that underpin the software services and platforms used within an AWS isolated region. **Top Reasons To Join Our Team** * Collaborate with and learn from the best networking engineers in the world * Get to develop, build and operate networks at the very largest scale, in an organisation that values its engineers and promotes innovation and ownership * Be part of a unique team that’s dedicated to history\-making programmes * Start a career path in one of the largest and most successful tech companies with a near\-endless opportunity to explore and grow Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. **Basic Qualifications** * Knowledge of IP networking fundamentals and extensive experience in the application of IP protocols * Knowledge of network analysis fundamentals and robust troubleshooting * Experience in major internet routing protocols, specifically BGP and OSPF * Experience with network operating systems such as Cisco IOS and Junos * This role requires you to be a national of an EU member state. **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of IT best practice frameworks (ITIL, COBIT) and operational excellence concepts or methodologies * Knowledge of network hardware and packet forwarding architectures * Experience with Unix and scripting in Perl, shell, C or C\+\+ * Experience working with customers to diagnose a problem and work towards resolution Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. title: Network Operations Engineer, GEN Operations
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in a technical field, or equivalent practical experience. * 3 years of experience with MPLS VPN, Firewalls, Data center Networking, and Virtual Extensible LAN (VXLAN). * Experience with core networking protocols and technologies, including TCP/IP, routing protocols (BGP, OSPF, ISIS), and LAN switching. * Experience coding in Python or Go to develop automation solutions for network monitoring and management. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Certifications: Professional or expert\-level industry certifications such as CCNP, CCIE, JNCIE, or Google's Professional Cloud Network Engineer certification. * Experience performing network troubleshooting and participating in global on\-call rotations to maintain service level objectives (SLOs). * Familiarity with Data Center Architecture, Wide Area Network (WAN) design, and Wireless Communications. * A solid understanding of Linux/Unix fundamentals, including system resource monitoring and shell scripting. * Demonstrated ability to manage projects with some complexity, contribute to technical documentation, and mentor junior team members. **About the job** ----------------- Google is proud to boast a network that provides service to millions of Internet users around the world. The Network Engineering team is responsible for operating that network reliably and at scale. As a member of the team, you have a direct impact on design and feature enhancements to keep our systems running smoothly. You also ensure that network operations are safe and efficient by monitoring network performance, coordinating planned maintenance, adjusting hardware components and responding to network connectivity issues. Google's complex network generates a constant stream of challenges which require you to continually be innovative with an evolving set of technologies. Keeping the network reliable ensures that our users stay connected with our suite of applications, products and services. In this role, you will operate Google Enterprise Network within Service Level Objective (SLO) to provide a reliable, secure and consistent networking experience to Googlers through effective monitoring, faster service restoration, efficient tools and processes.Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next\-generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €100000 \- €103000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Serve as a subject matter expert in multiple network domains (e.g., Routing, Switching, Wireless, Firewalls, SD\-WAN, Network Automation). * Manage the network escalations and provide technical leadership during critical service\-impacting events, working closely with the Network Operations Center (NOC). * Lead initiatives to meet and exceed Service Level Objectives (SLOs), aiming for a 99\.95% network availability target globally. * Oversee the operational aspects of New Product Introductions (NPIs), including designs like NextGEN WAN and Office, involving testing, defining operational requirements, developing support models, and creating documentation. * Develop scripts and tools (often using Python or Go) to automate network diagnostics, configuration, and monitoring tasks. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Ocean Freight Apprentice
