title: Options Sales Middle Office Analyst
description: **Overview** Joining the Client team as a Sales Operations Analyst supporting the Options Sales business, you will be responsible for Susquehanna’s options sales desk’s post trade processes to provide a seamless client experience. This role involves working closely with our sales traders, traders, and counterparties. Due to the high volume of trades, we are looking for candidates who have exceptional attention to detail. This role will offer a great exposure to a front office Sales \& Trading environment and will be an excellent opportunity if you have strong communication skills and an interest in financial markets. Being directly exposed to client interactions, a good level of commercial awareness is desired. **In This Role You Will** * Responsible for post trade processes * Execution and allocation of orders through internal/external systems * Act as a first point of contact for any issues related to crossing and settling trades across multiple exchanges, countries and clients * Address each client’s specific post trade requirements and tailor the process to their needs. For example, clients trading on CFD or making sure trades with FTT are accounted for correctly. * Identify opportunities to improve the overall process, automate tools where possible in order to enhance the broader desk’s performance and efficiency. This could include working with the static data team to improve our flex trading process and working with app support and developers to improve the desks tools * Liaise with other teams to develop and work on ad hoc projects aimed at improving the team’s workflow **What We're Looking For** * Minimum of a Bachelors’ degree * Ability to prioritise and complete multiple tasks in a fast paced, dynamic environment * High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Python a plus * Strong communicator (verbal and written) * Ability to work under pressure and multi\-task * Ambitious and self\-motivated, not afraid to ask questions and use own initiative * Effective problem solving and analytical skills; including effective troubleshooting in a fast\-paced, time\-critical environment * Experience in financial markets is not required although we welcome those with experience * Excellent attention to detail **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Order Fulfillment Exec
description: **Role Overview** The Order Fulfilment Executive is responsible for ensuring that all customer orders are processed, delivered, and invoiced efficiently and accurately. This role is critical to maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction and requires close collaboration with internal teams including Sales, Sales Support, Product, and Finance. **Key Responsibilities** * Accurately process customer sales orders, ensuring all details are reviewed and verified * Manage order processing through CRM and internal systems * Monitor open orders, track progress, and provide timely updates to account managers and customers * Coordinate the delivery and installation of services for customers * Identify and resolve any issues related to order fulfilment or service delivery * Provide administrative support across the business when required * Manage and respond to requests within a centralised inbox **Skills \& Experience** **Essential Requirements** * Understanding of order fulfilment processes and customer service best practices * Previous experience in order fulfilment, customer service, or a sales support/administrative environment * Experience using CRM systems and Microsoft Office tools * Strong data entry and analytical capabilities * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines * High level of accuracy and attention to detail **Behavioural Competencies** * Positive, proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and support colleagues and customers * Strong team player, comfortable working in a collaborative environment * Adaptable and open to change in processes, systems, and priorities * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced, evolving environment **Experience** * 2–3 years’ experience in a procurement, sales support, or related operational role **Reporting Line** * Reports to: Head of Sales Support * Direct line management: Sales Support Manager

title: Overnight Resident Experience Associate
description: **Role Summary** We are seeking a motivated Overnight Resident Experience Associate to join our team at Alta Verde, a premier Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) property located in Cross Avenue, Blackrock, Dublin. This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to progress their career in the growing BTR industry, working for a company that is expanding its footprint in Ireland. The successful candidate, reporting to the Property Manager, will be integral to daily property operations, focusing on resident relations, health and safety, cleanliness and security. This highly visible role will involve regular interaction with residents and visitors helping ensure high standards in resident engagement and satisfaction. We’re seeking candidates with strong experience in customer engagement. This job would ideally suit candidates from hospitality roles like: Security Operative, Night Auditor, Assistant Night Manager / Night Manager. CompassRock has a dedicated and comprehensive training and certification program, and this role will have the potential to progress into more managerial role over time. Some of the training programs include The Property Institute’s Build to Rent course and Managing Safely Qualifications. **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sector. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across England and Europe. CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, the company values the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs. As CompassRock continues to expand, there will be abundant opportunities for career advancement and professional development. **Key Responsibilities** **Customer Service \& Resident Relations:** * Policy and Standards Adherence: Uphold the company’s customer service standards to deliver high\-quality service and resident satisfaction. * Issue Resolution: Address resident complaints and actively troubleshoot problems to take ownership in challenging situations, escalating issues to the Property Manager when necessary. * Resident Engagement Initiatives: Support the planning and execution of community\-building activities that foster positive resident relations and engagement. * Resident Support: Provide residents with appropriate information throughout the tenancy lifespan, including providing renewal information, ensuring legal requirements are met and answering any questions that might occur during the tenancy lifespan. * Relationship Building: Cultivate strong relationships with residents, ensuring professional and prompt communication. Regularly engage with residents and prospective tenants to build strong rapport and trust. **Leasing Support:** Provide administrative support to the Leasing team, as required. * Enquiries: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties (if required). * Viewings: Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property (if required). * Administrative Support: Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. **Operational Activities** * Property Support: Contribute to maintaining a safe and attractive property for residents. Facilities Management Support: Assist the Property Manager with managing projects, overseeing outsourced services for building maintenance, and ensuring timely, high\-quality completion of tasks. * Cleanliness: Complete overnight cleaning of the amenity spaces, ensuring that the property is “resident ready” each day. * ‘Stock’ Coordination: Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. Completing stock checks on a monthly basis. * Inspections: Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * Administrative Support: Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties, as required. **Targets And KPIs** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities** * Attention to Detail: Demonstrated focus on quality and compliance in previous work or education (desired). * Proactive and Initiative\-Driven: Able to foresee potential issues and address them before they escalate (desired). Sharing suggestions on how to improve efficiencies, encourage proactivity and support your colleagues. * Flexibility: Ability to adapt to changing circumstances, particularly in a fast\-paced property environment (desired). * Positive, 'can\-do' Attitude: A willingness to learn from, and provide support to, other team members, while delivering top levels of service to all residents (essential). **Skills** * Communication Skills: Both written and verbal, for interacting with residents, staff, and external stakeholders (essential) * Problem\-Solving: Strong abilities in addressing resident disputes (essential). * Customer Service: Demonstrated experience in delivering exceptional customer service (essential). * Computer Literacy: While training on our property management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired)** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Full clean Irish driver’s licence **Remuneration/ Bonuses / Benefits** * Base salary:€37,400 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. **Working Hours** * 5\-day working week (40 hours) including weekends (Saturday and Sunday). * This role includes nighttime shift work (between 22:30 – 07:30\) **Location:** This is a client facing role and must be based on site at the rostered location(primarily at Alta Verde, Cross Avenue, Blackrock, Dublin. but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process.

title: PE and SPHE Teacher
company: Kilkenny And Carlow Education And Training Board
description: **Teaching and learning is the core focus of Kilkenny and Carlow ETB and we are committed to recruiting high quality teachers with appropriate qualifications, expertise and experience.** **Qualifications** ***It is an advantage if the candidate has a post graduate qualification in Special Education Needs/Learning Support or be in the process of applying for or completing this qualification.*** Applicants must meet the general and special requirements as set out from time to time by the Teaching Council of Ireland for the advertised subject(s). Further details and a full list of recognised qualifications are available on www.teachingcouncil.ie. Applicants who are in their final year of degree/postgraduate diploma which will lead to a teaching qualification, but who have not yet completed their final exams and/or received their results, may apply for advertised vacancies, subject to the provisions regarding Teaching Council registration set out below. Any offer of interview or employment is made pending the applicant’s results meeting the requirements of the vacancy. **Registration with the Teaching Council of Ireland (Essential)** Applicants must be currently registered with the Teaching Council of Ireland. Applicants shall provide a current Registration Certificate, or confirmation of Registration with their Application Form for a teaching position. **Teaching****Competences** Persons appointed to teaching positions within Kilkenny and Carlow ETB will be required to show evidence of the following key competences: * + Professional Knowledge + Professional Practice + Communication and relationship building + Self\-awareness and self\-management skills Candidates will be required to describe in no more than 300 words per competency some of their personal achievements to\-date that demonstrate the key competencies required for the position as outlined. Candidates should describe situation/s from their own experience, which they think are the best example/s of what they have done which demonstrates the specific competencies. The example(s) may be drawn from candidates experience in various settings including their professional, community or voluntary involvement. **Professional Knowledge** *Demonstrate in\-depth knowledge, comprehension and understanding of both the subject discipline and pedagogy* *Indicators include:* * Has a strong knowledge of subject matter. * Has an awareness and understanding of specific requirements of course/syllabus/specifications. * Keeps up to date with subject matter/course syllabus changes and developments in the education system. * Understands the cross\-curricular links to other content areas and is able to integrate learning across and between content areas (e.g. literacy, numeracy, critical thinking). * Knows, understands and critically evaluates the range of teaching and learning philosophies / theories and knows how to apply them appropriately within their subject (s). * Is aware of the range of supports available to support curriculum development and implementation. * Participates in and contributes to educational planning and development within the school e.g. lesson planning, subject department planning, etc. **Professional Practice** *Understands the nature of high quality teaching and learning and demonstrates the skills and competencies to illustrate these.* *Indicators include:* ***Planning and Preparation*** * Plans and communicates clear, challenging, appropriate and achievable expectations for students. * Shows knowledge and understanding of how to structure and schedule schemes of work, class plans, subject department plans etc. * Knows and understands the relationship between the planning, implementation and evaluation of the curriculum. * Selects, creates and pitches class materials which respond to the needs of students. **Teaching and Learning** * Understands the factors that contribute to a stimulating learning environment and the role of the teacher in supporting this environment. * Understands the practices that underpin effective classroom teaching and meet the learning styles of a diverse range of students e.g. differentiation, learning intentions, group work, etc. * Establishes standards of behaviour that enable all students to learn and deals with inappropriate behaviour in the context of school policies. * Demonstrates in\-depth knowledge and understanding of classroom management techniques and skills. * Effectively manages the range of behaviours/situations that can occur in the classroom in line with School Code of Behaviour maintaining good order and discipline among students. * Effectively uses a variety of teaching materials and technologies to actively engage students in the learning process e.g. collaboration, technology enhanced teaching and learning. ***Assessment*** * Focuses on assessment for learning by monitoring student progress, giving constructive feedback and helping students to reflect on and improve their learning e.g. formative and summative assessment. * Selects from a range of assessment strategies to evaluate student learning, and uses this information in their planning to help make their teaching more effective. * Assesses the levels of student attainment against relevant benchmarking data and understands the relationship between student assessment and target setting. * Liaises orally and in written reports in an effective manner with parents/guardians on student progress and achievement. **Communication and relationship building** *Establishes effective communication and engagement to ensure positive and effective working relationships with management, colleagues, students, parents and the wider school community.* *Indicators include:* * Seeks to foster positive relationships with students, colleagues, parents, school management and the wider school community in a professional, collaborative, supportive and respectful manner. * Communicates and contributes effectively and collaboratively within the school team environment. * Shows the capacity and skills to relate to and communicate effectively with students, parents, colleagues, school management and the wider school community. * Supports conflict resolution in all aspects of teaching role. * Motivates, inspires and celebrates student effort and success. * Is committed to equality and inclusion and to respecting and accommodating diversity. * Understands how to exercise a duty of care to students. * Participates in and supports the extra\-curricular programmes/activities of the school. * Contributes positively to the school community. **Self\-awareness and self\-management skills** *Is self\-aware and has the capacity to self\-manage and develop personally and professionally*. *Indicators include:* * Engages in reflective practice of their own work and consistently reviews it in order to ensure it is of the highest possible standard and responsive to changing teaching demands. * Maintains a strong focus on personal and professional growth including continual professional development, participation in professional learning communities, group reflection and practice. * Has the confidence, resilience and optimism to maintain an emotional balance in challenging situations and the capacity to work through these situations. * Is open and responsive to constructive feedback regarding their professional practice and, if necessary, seeks appropriate support, advice and guidance. * Is aware of the Teaching Council Code of Professional Conduct and Social Media Guidelines and is familiar with its professional requirements. * Practices within the statutory framework and regulations pertaining to education, including child protection and health and safety guidelines. * Upholds professional integrity for example discretion, confidentiality, loyalty and trust, empathy etc in all aspects of their professional role as a teacher. * Is aware of the broader student context and their requirement to model best practice in all aspect of their role and relationships in the school community. Please check your email (including spam folders) for updates. If you have any technical issues when applying for this position, please contact Jobtrain at; https://jobseekersupport.jobtrain.co.uk/support/home where you can log a ticket or call 0044 161 850 2004\. FAQ's are available in the top right menu once you have registered. (Please note Jobtrain hours are from 9am to 5\.30pm, and queries may take up to 24hrs to be answered).

title: Part-Time Front of House/Receptionist
description: **The Role / Location** The overall purpose of this role will be to manage the Reception and Front of House on a daily basis and provide a five\-star service to the corporate clients. This is a high\-profile reception role and requires the highest standard of corporate presentation and communication. This is a part\-time, 4 days per week role. **Key Responsibilities** Key responsibilities of the role include but are not limited to: * Carrying out all Reception and Front of House duties as part of the Front Office Team * Meeting and greeting visitors in a professional and welcoming manner, promoting a positive image at all times * Managing the switchboard and directing all calls efficiently, promptly, and professionally * Providing assistance and support to all visitors as required * Monitoring security cameras and promptly notifying the Facilities Manager of any security concerns or breaches * Managing and distributing incoming and outgoing post * Maintaining the reception area to a consistently high standard, ensuring it is clean, tidy, and well organised * Signing in and coordinating access for maintenance contractors in line with site procedures * Ensuring complete client satisfaction through the prompt and efficient handling of queries in a friendly and professional manner * Anticipating client and tenant needs and delivering service that meets and exceeds expectations **Person Specification** The successful candidate will: 1. Be highly customer\-focused, flexible, and extremely well organised 2. Enjoy working in a corporate, front of house environment 3. Demonstrate excellent communication and interpersonal skills 4. Present to a high standard in a corporate setting and have strong client relationship skills 5. Have the ability to multi\-task effectively while demonstrating excellent attention to detail **Key Competencies** 1. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills 2. Immaculate professional presentation 3. Previous experience in a Front of House or corporate reception role 4. A positive “can\-do” attitude, with the confidence to interact with a wide range of stakeholders **Savills Ireland is an equal opportunity employer.** *Savills aims to attract, recruit and retain the best people from the widest possible pool of talent. As such, we are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse working environment for all employees by striving at all times, to be an organisation which does not discriminate, values everyone’s talents and abilities and where diversity is positively promoted.*

title: Pharmacovigilance Officer (Hybrid)
description: TCP Homecare is a healthcare service provider, specialising in 'direct to patient services' which include the dispensing and distribution of pharmaceutical products, home care nursing services and sharps waste management, facilitating a unique turnkey solution for the delivery of hospital care in the patient's home. We are looking for pharmacovigilance officers who demonstrates a high degree of self\-motivation and the ability to adapt to change. We have both permanent and contract roles available. **This position is ideally suited to a recent graduate looking to gain experience in pharmacovigilance.** Key Responsibilities: * Process PV events, and product quality complaints in line the organisations operating procedures * Perform mailbox triage and management, case processing, reconciliation, query management and other tasks to ensure compliance with pharmacovigilance procedures. * Be involved in carrying out regular reconciliation of case reports with internal and external sources. * Ensure compliance with internal TCP SOPS and other relevant procedures where applicable * Maintain the Pharmacovigilance reporting system as well as provide systematic reporting to in\-house managers and external clients. * Dealing with external pharmaceutical departments and other multidisciplinary teams. * Maintain customer confidentiality at all times. * Assist in the development of pharmacovigilance procedures. * Act as a key information resource to pharmacovigilance team and other multidisciplinary teams. * In compliance with client requirements ensure that all service user records and files are managed, maintained and up to date * Create incidents reports that occur in PV and drive the actions that are required remediate errors. This may require liaising directly with other functional leads to align processes to PV standards. * Adhere to GVP (good pharmacovigilance practices) guidelines at all times. * Perform other duties as assigned by PV Manager * Represents self in a professional and ethical manner at all times, complying with all applicable company policy and procedures Role requirements: * Degree in life sciences or working towards, highly desirable. * High degree of accuracy with attention to detail essential. * Familiarity with good pharmacovigilance practices (GVP) desirable. * Knowledge of medical and drug terminology would be a distinct advantage. * Good written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work independently with moderate supervision. * Good knowledge and experience using MS Office would be beneficial. **What We Offer in Return:** Competitive annual salary 24 days of annual leave, rising with service Pension Scheme Life Assurance Health Insurance Income Protection Education Assistance funding with paid exam and study Leave Employee Discount Card **You Can Also Expect:** A supportive and friendly working environment Regular training courses and opportunities to up\-skill Promotional opportunities Refer a Friend scheme Social events Cycle to Work scheme

title: Platform Engineer
description: **Platform Engineer (AWS Cloud Infrastructure)** **12\-month Contract** **About the Company** Our client is a fast\-growing financial services organisation focused on building secure, resilient, and scalable cloud\-native platforms that support the next generation of digital financial products and services. Technology sits at the core of their business strategy, with significant investment in cloud infrastructure, automation, security, and engineering excellence. Operating within a highly regulated environment, the organisation is committed to delivering innovative solutions while maintaining the highest standards of security, governance, and operational resilience **.** **About the Role** We are seeking an experienced Platform Engineer to join a high\-performing cloud platform team responsible for designing, building, and supporting modern AWS\-based infrastructure environments. This is a hands\-on engineering role focused on cloud architecture, infrastructure automation, platform reliability, and security. The successful candidate will play a key role in building scalable cloud platforms, implementing Infrastructure as Code, and supporting secure software delivery pipelines. Working closely with engineering, security, and operations teams, you will help deliver well\-architected solutions that align with industry best practices and regulatory requirements. This position is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex infrastructure challenges and contributing to the evolution of cloud\-native platforms. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, build, and maintain secure, scalable, and resilient AWS cloud infrastructure * Implement and manage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) solutions using Terraform * Develop and maintain automation frameworks for cloud provisioning, deployment, and management * Support cloud networking, security, and governance initiatives across AWS environments * Design and manage multi\-account AWS architectures, landing zones, and cloud governance frameworks * Collaborate with engineering teams to build and optimise CI/CD pipelines and GitOps workflows * Implement security best practices including IAM, encryption, key management, and access controls * Monitor platform health, performance, reliability, and availability across cloud environments * Support incident management, troubleshooting, and root\-cause analysis activities * Contribute to cloud architecture decisions and platform strategy initiatives * Drive continuous improvement across automation, security, scalability, and operational processes * Work closely with security and compliance teams to ensure adherence to regulatory standards **Key Requirements** **Essential Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years of experience in Platform Engineering, Cloud Engineering, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), or Software Engineering * Minimum 3 years of hands\-on AWS experience * Proven experience designing and supporting production\-grade cloud infrastructure * Strong understanding of AWS networking, security, identity management, and governance * Advanced networking knowledge including firewalls, DNS, load balancing, proxies, and network security * Strong experience with Infrastructure as Code, particularly Terraform * Experience with CI/CD pipelines, automation frameworks, and GitOps methodologies * Proficiency in at least one scripting or programming language such as Python, Go, or Shell * Experience implementing cloud security best practices, including IAM, encryption, and KMS * Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across technical teams **Desirable Skills \& Experience** * Experience within financial services, fintech, insurance, or other regulated industries * Experience with multi\-account AWS environments, Control Tower, and landing zone implementations * Knowledge of Zero Trust security architectures * Hands\-on experience with Docker and Kubernetes/EKS * Experience with monitoring and observability tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, Splunk, Dynatrace, or AWS\-native services * Experience optimising cloud cost, performance, and operational efficiency * Exposure to cloud architecture and platform strategy initiatives * Participation in cloud engineering communities or open\-source projects

title: Platform Engineer
description: **About Smartbox:** We exist to be the go\-to companion, empowering everyone to transform their free time into experiences. At Smartbox Group, we believe that real\-life experiences help us thrive, connect, and become the best versions of ourselves. With nearly 15,000 different products across leisure themes — Stays, Wellness, Adventure, Gastronomy — we allow people to enrich their lives with moments of sharing, emotions, and unforgettable memories. Present in 11 countries, Smartbox Group employs over 700 people across Europe and works with more than 40,000 trusted partners, including hotels, restaurants, wellness centres, and outdoor activity providers. **Our Culture:** At Smartbox, we’re on a mission to unbox our world and unbox the potential of our People. We’re not just building a workplace — we’re cultivating a movement where bold ideas, authentic connections, and real\-world experiences redefine what work can be. We empower our people to take ownership, break boundaries, and dive headfirst into challenges that spark growth and impact. Together, we’re creating a culture of daring curiosity, relentless ambition, and transformative purpose — where every team member shapes their future and leaves a lasting mark on the world around us. **The Role:** We are looking for a Platform Engineer to join our Technical Operations team in Dublin, reporting to the Platform Engineering Manager. Platform Engineers at Smartbox are responsible for keeping all user\-facing and production systems running smoothly 24/7/365, constantly looking to improve workflows and processes to deliver best\-in\-class experiences to our customers and internal stakeholders. In this role, you will participate in our on\-call rotation, partner with development teams to improve their services and processes, and help maintain and advance our technology stack. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design, implement, and maintain cloud infrastructure in AWS using Terraform, including development of reusable modules. * Operate and enhance production Kubernetes clusters using Helm, Karpenter, ArgoCD, and AWS EKS. * Implement and maintain GitOps\-driven CI/CD pipelines for infrastructure and application deployments. * Improve platform reliability through proactive monitoring, alerting, and observability using Prometheus, Grafana, CheckMK, and internal tools. * Automate operational processes using Bash, Python, Go, and Rust. * Troubleshoot production incidents across infrastructure, networking, containers, and application layers. * Support and optimise large\-scale, database\-driven systems (MySQL required; PostgreSQL a plus). * Contribute to capacity planning, scaling strategies, and performance optimisation initiatives. * Drive operational excellence improvements and incident reduction initiatives. * Manage and optimise centralised logging using Elasticsearch, Filebeat, rsyslog, and Kibana. * Maintain documentation and participate in a 24x7 on\-call rotation. * Collaborate with development teams on tools development, service improvements, and change management activities. **About You:** We’re looking for someone who not only has the right skills but also embodies our guiding principles: * **The Owner** — You take accountability, act with integrity, and treat challenges as opportunities. * **The Ambitious Maverick** — You bring bold ideas, challenge the status quo, and are unafraid to take smart risks. * **The Radical Optimist** — You maintain a solutions\-focused mindset, inspiring others with your positivity and resilience. * **The Connector** — You build authentic relationships, foster collaboration, and create space for diverse voices to thrive. **Skills \& Experience:** **Essential:** * 3\+ years of experience in technical support, systems administration, or platform/infrastructure engineering. * Proficiency in LAMP/LNMP environments. * Hands\-on experience with Kubernetes and Docker. * Experience with modern CI/CD tooling (GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or equivalent). * Strong Linux systems knowledge and troubleshooting capability. * Solid understanding of networking fundamentals (DNS, TCP/IP, HTTP, load balancing, TLS, caching). * Experience with cloud automation on AWS and Terraform. * Experience with Elasticsearch and log management. * Scripting skills in Python, Bash, or similar languages. * Experience administering MySQL databases. * Excellent troubleshooting and analytical skills. **Desirable:** * Experience with configuration management tools such as Chef/Cinc. * PostgreSQL database experience. * Experience leveraging AI tools to improve code quality and establishing AI\-related workflows. **What we offer:** 🏡 Hybrid work: 2–3 remote days \+ 20 days abroad per year ✅ Essentials: health cover, pension, taxsaver benefit, extra leave 🎁 Flexible benefits: personalize with health, wellbeing \& learning options 🌈 D\&I commitment: parental leave, Inclusive Day, gender equity in rewards L\&D Support: Study Assistance and Study Leave 🎉 Unbox Your World: Take 1 day leave per year as short notice (YOLO!) \+ paid sabbatical after 5 years 🌍 Transparency: clear, fair salary bands and career levels

title: Power Systems Engineer
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** ELSE Cluster Manager North Europe \_\_ **Your role and responsibilities** In this role you will work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment, developing technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry\-recognized consulting engineer. The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive, innovative electrical consultancy. We are seeking experienced Power Systems Engineers who are eager to deepen their technical expertise and contribute to complex engineering projects for global clients. This role is ideal for a practical, adaptable, and technically driven engineer who wants to grow into an industry\-recognized consulting specialist within a progressive and innovative electrical consultancy environment. The work model for the role is: \#hybrid, \#Li\-hybrid You will be mainly accountable for: * Providing technical input and leadership on a variety of complex projects. * Working closely with clients to understand their technical and business requirements and translating them into practical engineering solutions. * Ensuring project outputs meet client expectations, quality standards, timeframes, and budget requirements. * Conducting in\-depth power systems analysis using industry\-leading methodologies and software tools. * Preparing detailed technical and non\-technical reports, presenting clear recommendations and actionable insights. * Attending client meetings remotely, building trusted relationships and managing expectations through effective communication and responsiveness. * Tracking and reporting on project performance and client satisfaction. * Mentoring, coaching and developing more junior engineers. Online training to be delivered where applicable. **Typical projects include:** * Power system studies include load flow, short circuit, protection coordination and system resilience assessments. * Electrical safety and power quality studies, including arc flash, harmonics and earthing assessments. * Grid connection and renewable energy integration projects, covering stability, harmonics and compliance analyses. * Reliability assessments and failure investigations for critical electrical infrastructure. * Software\-based engineering solutions and emerging technology evaluations. * Supporting wind energy projects from design through installation and commissioning. **Qualifications for the role** * You are highly skilled in power system studies, including short circuit, protection coordination and arc flash analysis, and enjoy working on complex electrical infrastructure projects. * You have 3\+ years of experience in power systems engineering, applying technical expertise to deliver reliable, safe and efficient electrical solutions. * You possess advanced knowledge of industry\-leading power system analysis tools such as SKM, EasyPower, ETAP or similar. * You have proven experience in protection system design, settings development and coordination studies, with exposure to transformer and bus differential protection, REF and directional protection schemes. * You have experience performing electrical calculations and developing designs in accordance with relevant IEC standards. * Knowledge of power quality, harmonics and renewable energy integration studies would be an advantage. * You are passionate about solving technical challenges, collaborating with clients and stakeholders, and continuously expanding your expertise. * Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field. * You are at ease communicating in English and can clearly present technical concepts to a wide range of audiences Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Primary Education Officer
description: Post\-Primary Languages Ireland (PPLI) are currently recruiting for a Primary Education Officer to join their team. **About The Role:** =================== Post\-Primary Languages Ireland/Languages Connect is seeking to recruit an Education Officer with competence in a foreign language to support the implementation of Languages Connect – Ireland’s Strategy for Foreign Languages in Education, diversifying, enhancing and expanding the teaching of foreign languages. The candidate for this role will be required to be qualified as a primary school teacher, have excellent leadership and interpersonal skills, be able to motivate and inspire teachers and work with teams of associates. Flexibility and an ability to engage in an evolving programme of work in a culture of improvement, collaboration, innovation, and creativity are required. **Key Requirements:** --------------------- Applicants must (on the latest date for receipt of completed applications): * Have in\-depth understanding of the Irish education system, the Languages Connect strategy and the Primary Language Curriculum * Hold a qualification to teach at primary level * Have relevant experience in primary level education * Have experience and expertise in the use of active learning methodologies and other up\-to\-date pedagogies and approaches to teaching and learning of languages * Have a proven record of managing complex projects, meeting deadlines, and achieving results * Possess a minimum level of CEFR B2 in at least one foreign language * Have excellent communication and presentation skills and the ability to write to a very high standard * Be proficient in Microsoft Office ### **Desirable Skills and Qualifications:** * Hold a qualification in a foreign language and/or a relevant qualification; * Irish language skills to carry out work through the medium of Irish; * Have familiarity with CEFR and CEFR Companion Volume ### **Required Competencies:** * Project management (including planning \& organisation, ability to multi\-task, prioritise where necessary, flexibility, working independently and as part of a team) * Judgement, analysis and decision making * Management and delivery of Results * Leadership, including interpersonal skills, motivating/inspiring others both within the team and with external stakeholders * Research and Report writing * Interpersonal and communication skills, including competence to engage stakeholders and partners * Professional knowledge, expertise, and self\-development, including digital expertise and capacity to prepare and deliver workshops, training and presentations **Key Responsibilities:** ------------------------- * Prepare detailed breakdown of deliverables and deadlines for projects * Oversee and manage the achievement of deliverables of projects * Manage, develop and enhance the Foreign Language Upskilling Scheme, by engaging with a range of providers, including Education Support Centres, HEIs, Cultural Institutes and Embassies * Evaluate the impact of programmes * Provide advice and training to tutors recruited to deliver upskilling courses, where relevant * Maintain effective communication with all course providers and teachers/schools * Deliver information sessions to hosting class teachers and school management teams in the context of Say Yes to Languages * Develop, prepare, and deliver resources and supports for teaching and learning to support delivery of MFL curriculum * Assess teachers’ needs via research and surveys * Establish and maintain working relationships with schools, Department of Education and all other relevant stakeholders * Manage training of, and collaboration with, associates * Support the delivery of messages to the wider public through articles, presentations and newsletter items. Prepare project reports and conduct evaluation of programmes * Represent PPLI/Languages Connect at events such as conferences, give presentations, facilitate workshops ***Deadline for applications is 4pm on Friday, 10th July 2026\.*** For more information on the role, please see the Information Booklet. To apply, please fill out the Application Form. **About PPLI:** =============== Post\-Primary Languages Ireland (PPLI) provides expertise and support for foreign language education in Ireland, fully funded by the Department of Education. Along with a growing number of partners, PPLI also delivers the Languages Connect awareness campaign, which highlights the benefits of foreign language skills.

title: Private Equity Investment Associate – Exceptional Platform & Career Development
description: **Job Title:** Private Equity Investment Associate – Exceptional Platform \& Career Development **Sector** : Private Equity **Location:** Dublin **Salary:** DOE plus bonus and benefits **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded Private Equity investor with a long\-standing track record of partnering with ambitious businesses and supporting management teams through periods of significant growth and transformation. Due to continued investment activity and a strong deal pipeline, they are seeking to appoint an Investment Associate to join their Dublin team. **Why should you apply?** The role offers exposure across all aspects of the investment lifecycle, with Associates playing a hands\-on role from origination through to execution and portfolio value creation. title: Product Designer - Germany (m/w/d)
description: **Möchtest du das Leben von Millionen Unternehmer in Europa verändern?** ======================================================================== Du möchtest echten Impact haben und die Zukunft von Finanzen und Buchhaltung in Europa mitgestalten? Du fühlst dich in einem dynamischen Umfeld wohl, das auf Vertrauen, Eigenverantwortung und Geschwindigkeit setzt? Und unsere Design\-Prinzipien entsprechen deiner Vorstellung von großartigen Nutzererlebnissen? Dann könnte Pennylane genau der richtige Ort für dich sein – und du genau die richtige Person für diese Rolle. **Unsere Vision** ----------------- Wir bauen das beliebteste Financial Operating System für kleine und mittelständische Unternehmen in Europa. Unser Ziel ist es, Unternehmer von zeitaufwendigen Aufgaben rund um Buchhaltung und Finanzen zu entlasten und ihnen gleichzeitig Zugang zu den wichtigsten Finanzinformationen zu geben, damit sie bessere Entscheidungen für ihr Unternehmen treffen können. Gleichzeitig unterstützen wir Steuerkanzleien dabei, weniger Zeit für repetitive Aufgaben aufzuwenden und sich stärker auf die Beratung und Betreuung ihrer Mandant zu konzentrieren. Bereits heute nutzen mehr als 1\.000\.000 kleine und mittelständische Unternehmen sowie über 6\.500 Steuerkanzleien Pennylane in Frankreich. **Über uns** ------------ Pennylane gehört zu den am schnellsten wachsenden FinTechs in Frankreich – und bald auch in Deutschland. In nur fünf Jahren haben wir: * uns als innovative Accounting\- und Finanzplattform für Unternehmen und Steuerkanzleien etabliert, * insgesamt 150 Millionen Euro Kapital eingesammelt – unter anderem von Sequoia, dem renommierten Silicon\-Valley\-Investor hinter Unternehmen wie Google, Facebook, Airbnb, Stripe und PayPal, * unser Team von 7 Gründer auf über 1\.100 Pennylaners ausgebaut, * eine internationale Unternehmenskultur mit mehr als 25 Nationalitäten geschaffen, * eine starke Remote\-First\-Kultur etabliert, in der bereits rund 30 % unserer Mitarbeitenden von überall in Europa arbeiten, * das Vertrauen tausender Kund und Steuerkanzleien gewonnen und hervorragende Bewertungen erhalten. Heute gelten wir mit einer Glassdoor\-Bewertung von 4,6/5 als einer der attraktivsten Arbeitgeber Europas. **Pennylane Deutschland** ------------------------- Innerhalb von nur 1,5 Jahren ist unser Deutschland\-Team von einer einzelnen Squad auf über 40 Mitarbeitende in den Bereichen Product, Engineering, Sales, Customer Success und Marketing gewachsen. Unser Ziel ist es, Pennylane als festen Bestandteil des deutschen Marktes zu etablieren. Gemeinsam mit innovativen Steuerberater und Unternehmen entwickeln wir unser Produkt für die Anforderungen des deutschen Marktes weiter und verändern nachhaltig die Art und Weise, wie Buchhaltung und Finanzen in Deutschland funktionieren. Als eigenständiger Markt profitieren wir von der Unterstützung unserer französischen Teams und genießen gleichzeitig große Freiheiten bei der Gestaltung unserer Ziele, Strukturen, Prozesse und Produkte. **Design bei Pennylane** ======================== Design steht im Zentrum unserer Mission. Unser Anspruch ist es, Nutzer ein nahtloses, zugängliches und begeisterndes Produkterlebnis zu bieten. Unser Design\-Team besteht aus über 40 Product Designer, die in interdisziplinären Squads eng mit Product und Engineering zusammenarbeiten. Autonomie, Vertrauen und Transparenz bilden das Fundament unserer Unternehmenskultur und prägen den Arbeitsalltag unserer Designer. Bei Pennylane beschränkt sich Design nicht auf die Gestaltung von Interfaces. Designer begleiten den gesamten Prozess – von der Definition des Problems über Research und Testing bis hin zur Umsetzung der Lösung. **Deine Rolle** --------------- Wir suchen eine Product Designer mit fundierter Erfahrung im B2B\-Produktdesign, um die Weiterentwicklung unseres Produkts in Deutschland aktiv mitzugestalten. Wenn du gerne reale Nutzerprobleme löst, in einem kollaborativen Umfeld arbeitest und ein echtes Interesse daran hast, Nutzerverhalten und Geschäftsziele zu verstehen, wirst du bei Pennylane viele Gleichgesinnte finden. **Das bringst du mit** ---------------------- * Du verstehst sowohl Nutzerbedürfnisse als auch Business\-Anforderungen und kannst Erkenntnisse aus User Research, Marktanalysen und Daten in pragmatische Lösungen übersetzen. * Du hast Erfahrung mit komplexen digitalen Produkten und Systemen, beispielsweise mit mehreren Modulen, umfangreichen Funktionen oder komplexen Datenstrukturen. * Du arbeitest eng mit Entwickler, Product Manager und weiteren Stakeholdern wie Sales oder Support zusammen und kannst technische Rahmenbedingungen sowie Business\-Ziele in deine Arbeit integrieren. * Du kommunizierst klar, hinterfragst bestehende Annahmen konstruktiv und kannst deine Lösungsansätze überzeugend vertreten. * Du sprichst fließend Deutsch und Englisch. User Interviews werden regelmäßig auf Deutsch durchgeführt. **Was dich bei Pennylane erwartet** =================================== **Innerhalb des ersten Monats** ------------------------------- * Du lernst unser Unternehmen, unsere Teams und unsere Vision während des Onboardings kennen. * Du erhältst einen tiefen Einblick in unser Produkt sowie die Welt der Buchhaltung und Finanzen. * Du machst dich mit unserer Product\- und Design\-Organisation, unseren Methoden und unserer Kultur vertraut. * Du lernst die Menschen kennen, mit denen du täglich zusammenarbeiten wirst – sowohl innerhalb des Deutschland\-Teams als auch im internationalen Umfeld. **Innerhalb der ersten drei Monate** ------------------------------------ * Du führst Discovery\-Workshops und User Research durch und analysierst Daten, Nutzerfeedback und Business\-Anforderungen. * Gemeinsam mit deinem Product Manager verantwortest du die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung deines Produktbereichs. * Du entwickelst Lösungen in der passenden Detailtiefe – von ersten Konzepten bis hin zu High\-Fidelity\-Prototypen. * Du bringst deine Erfahrung aktiv ins Team ein, gibst Feedback an Kolleg und arbeitest an bereichsübergreifenden Themen mit. **Innerhalb der ersten sechs Monate** ------------------------------------- * Du unterstützt aktiv beim Ausbau unseres Teams und wirkst im Recruiting\-Prozess mit. * Du beobachtest aktuelle UX\- und UI\-Trends und bringst Best Practices aus der internationalen Tech\-Welt ein. **Und darüber hinaus** ---------------------- Sowohl unser Design\-Team als auch unsere Aktivitäten in Deutschland werden weiter wachsen. Das bedeutet für dich: * Möglichkeiten, neue Designer einzustellen, zu coachen und perspektivisch zu führen. * Mehr Verantwortung für strategische Projekte. * Die Chance, Prozesse, Tools und Best Practices aktiv mitzugestalten und weiterzuentwickeln. **Das bieten wir dir** ====================== * Arbeite aus unserem Büro im Herzen Berlins, aus unserem Standort in Hamburg, aus unseren Büros in Paris oder aus jedem WeWork\-Standort in Europa. * Bei einem Full\-Remote\-Vertrag erhältst du ein Budget für dein Home Office sowie einen monatlichen Zuschuss für Coworking\-Spaces. * Zusätzlich zu 25 Urlaubstagen erhältst du jährlich zwischen 8 und 13 weitere freie Tage. * Zugang zu Busuu, um dein Englisch zu verbessern oder eine neue Sprache zu lernen. * Modernste Apple\-Hardware für deinen Arbeitsalltag. * Eine lebendige Community mit gemeinsamen Sportaktivitäten wie Fußball, Laufen oder Klettern. * Regelmäßige Team\-Events, After\-Work\-Abende und unsere beliebten Rooftop\-Drinks in Berlin. * Ein jährliches Company Offsite – zuletzt waren wir gemeinsam im Center Parcs und hatten eine großartige Zeit. **Unser Bewerbungsprozess** =========================== * Portfolio Review * Erstgespräch mit Sonia oder Barbara (Technical Recruiting) – 30 Minuten * Interview mit zwei Mitgliedern unseres Design\-Teams – 45 bis 60 Minuten * Take\-Home Assessment inklusive Präsentation und Review mit zwei weiteren Designer – 60 Minuten * Finales Gespräch mit unserem Chief Product Officer und dem Head of Product Germany – 60 Minuten **Vielfalt und Chancengleichheit** ---------------------------------- Bei Pennylane fördern wir Vielfalt in all ihren Formen und schaffen ein inklusives, wertschätzendes und erfüllendes Arbeitsumfeld – sowohl im Büro als auch remote. Wir bieten allen Bewerber die gleichen Chancen – unabhängig von Herkunft, Nationalität, Geschlecht, Religion, sexueller Orientierung, Alter oder Behinderung. **Données personnelles** Pennylane traite vos données pour gérer votre candidature et évaluer votre adéquation au poste. Si votre candidature n’aboutit pas, vos données peuvent être conservées 2 ans à compter de notre dernier échange ou de la clôture du recrutement afin de constituer \& gérer un vivier de candidats. Vous pouvez vous opposer à tout moment et demander la suppression de vos données en écrivant à : **dpo@pennylane.com.** Notre data policy **Personal Data** Pennylane processes your data to manage your application and assess your suitability for the position. If your application is unsuccessful, your data may be retained for 2 years from our last exchange or the closing of the recruitment process, in order to build and manage a candidate pool. You may object at any time and request the deletion of your data by writing to **dpo@pennylane.com**. Learn more **Personenbezogene Daten** Pennylane verarbeitet Ihre Daten, um Ihre Bewerbung zu bearbeiten und Ihre Eignung für die Stelle zu beurteilen. Sollte Ihre Bewerbung nicht erfolgreich sein, können Ihre Daten bis zu 2 Jahre ab unserem letzten Austausch oder dem Abschluss des Rekrutierungsverfahrens gespeichert werden, um einen Kandidatenpool aufzubauen und zu verwalten. Sie können jederzeit Widerspruch einlegen und die Löschung Ihrer Daten beantragen, indem Sie an **dpo@pennylane.com** schreiben. Mehr erfahren

title: Product Owner (Crypto)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. title: Project Coordination Specialist - Renewable Energy

* Degree qualified in Engineering, Business, Project Management or a related discipline * Minimum 1 year's experience supporting the delivery of large\-scale infrastructure, construction, utilities or energy projects * Strong organisational and project coordination skills * Excellent written communication, reporting and stakeholder engagement abilities * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Excel and Project * Experience using document management platforms such as SharePoint, ProjectWise or similar would be advantageous Experience Required; * Renewable Energy * Utilities * Infrastructure Delivery * Capital Projects * Project Controls * PMO Environments * Engineering Consultancy * Construction Project Delivery Professional project management qualifications such as PMP, PRINCE2 or equivalent would be advantageous. **What's on Offer?** * Opportunity to work on nationally significant renewable energy and infrastructure projects * Exposure to large\-scale project delivery and PMO best practice * Collaborative and supportive team environment * Strong learning and career development opportunities * Hybrid working model * Competitive salary and benefits package * Opportunity to contribute directly to Ireland's Net Zero ambitions **Contact** For more information on this position or other engineering roles, please contact Cathal on 01\-5927850 or hit the apply button below to send your cv. title: Project Engineer
description: **Overview** **About RoviSys:** RoviSys is an independent system integrator with year\-over\-year growth in Ireland, Europe, and world\-wide. RoviSys provides a variety of information and automation services in many industries globally. RoviSys Ireland formed in 2022, with the Dublin Office open in May 2024\. Dublin is part of RoviSys Europe group and currently operates closely with our European HQ located in Utrecht in The Nederland’s. Manufactures and systems we are currently working on in Ireland are including but not limited to Emerson DeltaV, Rockwell, Siemens PCS7 and S7, Schneider, Honeywell IQSET and Trend PLCs. SCADA include, Ignition, Aveva, AVEVA PI, IQ Vision, Cimplicity, and iFIX. **About The Opportunity** Project Engineers are responsible for leading engineering teams that design and implement customer automation projects. Project engineers take a comprehensive approach to solving problems, learning the details of each project’s mechanical, electrical, and software systems. **Responsibilities** **Software Design \& Development:** * Leading design and develop control system software for PLCs, DCS, SCADA, MES and historian platforms. * Create and maintain detailed design documentation to support system architecture and functionality. * Assists Project Manager in identifying changes in project scope and developing change orders * Serves as a technical contact for customers **Hardware Design \& Testing** * Design electrical control panels and oversee their testing to ensure compliance with specifications and safety standards. **Project Execution \& Validation** * Lead and conduct Factory Acceptance Testing to validate system performance prior to deployment. * Lead site commissioning activities for both hardware and software components. * Perform Site Acceptance Testing to confirm system functionality in the live environment. * On site commissioning A Project Engineer will typically work in a project team of 2 – 20 and will be expected to work closely with the project manager. Site location can vary, currently are in Ireland, Netherlands, Germany, UK, Spain, Nordic’s and more across the EU. As RoviSys operates a platform\-independent model, we engage with a wide variety of industrial systems. This creates a unique opportunity to work in a diverse and fast\-paced environment where continuous development and growth are part of everyday work. **Qualifications** * Bachelor of Science (Level 8\) in Engineering, Engineering Technology, Computer Science or and equivalent qualification. * 5\+ years of experience in automation * Eligible to work and travel in Ireland and Europe without sponsorship. * Full Irish Drivers License * Attend the Dublin Office on a daily basis * Strong background in industrial automation and control systems. * Hands\-on experience with PLC/DCS programming and SCADA configuration. * Familiarity with validation protocols and quality standards in regulated industries. * Experience in leading a team of engineers in a project environment * Excellent problem\-solving ability * Strong communication skills. * As a minimum, you must be proficient on one or more of the following platforms: + Rockwell ControlLogix/CompactLogix, PlantPAx, or FactoryTalk + Emerson DeltaV + Schneider Electric Modicon PLCs, EcoStruxure, PowerSCADA, Power Monitoring Expert (PME) + Siemens S7, PCS7, or WinCC + Ignition + Or similar IT, automation, or MES platform Open to travel is a must.

title: QA Engineer - Fuze Services
description: At Fuze Health, we put patients first and tirelessly address the most pressing needs in healthcare. We empower millions to digitally connect with care providers, essential health resources and needed treatments – and enable care providers, employers, health plans and life sciences companies to meaningfully enhance quality, outcomes and value. We are dedicated to helping our partners evolve and modernize to meet emerging patient and marketplace needs. Fuze Health’s foundation is built upon the strategic combination of several proven, technology\-powered innovators in the digital health, diagnostics, and pharmacy sectors. Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next\-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. Our QA Engineers take pride in ensuring the quality of our products that drive our core mission of empowering individuals, corporations, universities, public sector, and health insurance companies to use technology in a simple but powerful way to give them greater control of their healthcare needs. This role supports our products, operations, and growth with the tools and solutions to meet market demand. You will be at the intersection of product engineering, QA automation, and complex data environments. You will be deeply experienced in at least two, passionate about all three, and conversant with stakeholders in all. You will join us in ensuring the quality of our large scale, distributed platform spanning across multiple geographic locations. Our platform resides fully in AWS, operates in dozens of markets globally and focuses on ease of use for our customers along with integrity \& security for the data they trust us with. **Position Description** * Research, develop and apply new innovative technologies to increase the quality of our products * Be an advocate of state\-of\-the\-art QA automation technology and engineering approaches * You will work as part of a close\-knit product engineering team * Drive the quality of our new products and solutions that transform our industry * Develop test frameworks and automated tests for our core services, UI’s, and API’s using the best industry standards for mission critical services * Work with autonomy to find solutions to complex problems and be flexible and responsive to changing requirements and priorities * Be a team member on a variety of projects including new product / component development, proofs of concept, and reengineering initiatives among others * The ability for critical thinking outside your immediate sphere of responsibility * Have experience with commercial products and environments (not just internally focused) * Have a collaborative working style and contribute to the development of our technical communities across the company **What We Expect From You** * BS or MS in Computer Science (or related technical discipline) or equivalent industry experience * 3\+ years hands\-on experience designing and maintaining UI (responsive web apps) and API test automation frameworks * Strong experience writing tests in an OO language (preferably C\#) * Experience integrating test tools with automated CI/CD pipelines on AWS * Expertise communicating and maintaining test results, including dashboard visualizations * Comfortable providing input on issues that impact quality, product performance and system stability/reliability * Demonstrable experience creating test cases from requirements and providing recommendations for new methods and criteria to be tested * Experience in UI Automation Frameworks (preferably Playwright or Cypress) * Strong interest or experience in leveraging AI\-powered testing tools and LLMs * Experience in RestAssured, Postman or other API testing frameworks * Experience with full lifecycle software development, the associated tools, processes and methodologies * An excellent communicator (in English), proactive and self\-sufficient, with strong organizational, time management and problem\-solving skills * Proven ability to break down and clearly define problems * Ability to establish credibility and build relationships at all levels * Passion for best quality practice and continuous professional development **Our Preferred Qualifications** * Experience using Selenium * Experience working in geo\-distributed Cloud environments (preferably AWS) * Experience testing data science and business intelligence capabilities * Experience with container technologies, e.g. Docker, Kubernetes * Experience of data management environments (e.g. SQL Server, T\-SQL, NoSQL, Hadoop, Kafka, Spark, Elastic etc) * Knowledge of Information Security concepts * US Healthcare experience **Benefits** Alongside a base salary range of €47,900 to €64,900 we offer a range of benefits including: * Health insurance and an Employee Assistance Programme * Pension * Annual Compensation Reviews * Free monthly LetsGetChecked tests as we are not only focused on the well being of our patients but also the well being of our teams * A referral bonus programme to reward you for helping us hire the best talent * Internal Opportunities and Careers Clinics to help you progress your career within the company * Maternity, Paternity, Parental and Wedding leave * LetsGetChecked has a flexible annual leave policy At Fuze Health, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Please read our Candidate Privacy Notice which explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal information about job applicants during the recruitment and hiring process.

title: QA Engineer - Salon Ops (Reporting) 6 Month Maternity Cover
description: **About Phorest** Would you like to create the future of a product used by thousands of businesses and millions of consumers? Phorest Salon Software powers over 11,000 hair and beauty salons in Ireland, UK, Germany, Australia, Finland, USA, Canada, UAE and Netherlands and the platform processes 7 million appointments a month for hair and beauty consumers. Phorest is the number 1 salon software brand for premium independent salons in those markets and our next goal is to become a platform upon which other companies can build applications for premium salons. Our system touches every point of the salon experience. We have the in\-salon software desktop application, Phorest Go (the native staff application), online bookings website for salons, and custom native apps per salon. In a typical month, Phorest processes 3 million appointments and we send over 3 million SMS and 4 million emails. We process 200k online bookings per month and over 150 custom built white label native apps. ****The Opportunity**** Quality is critical within the company to ensure customer satisfaction and retention, driving growth, and helping clients extract maximum value from our products. We are looking for a QA Engineer (6 month maternity cover) to join our Reporting team within the Salon Ops pillar. This group builds and runs the core salon workflows that keep salons operating smoothly day to day. It’s made up of cross‑functional teams including Front of House, Phorest Go, Salon Management, and Integrations \& Partnerships. You will be a key member of the new Reporting team, automating test suits and bulletproofing the future of our world\-class e\-commerce products. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who wants a new challenge, where you’ll bring your experience to a supportive, growth mindset environment that will enable us to achieve our goals. ****What You'll Do**** * Be hands on testing new features and performing regression tests for various services + Design, develop and execute both manual and automated tests to verify the functionality of the system + Develop regression automated test suites + Identify, report and track issues to resolution + Help developers reproduce errors, and ensure that defects are fixed * Work closely with product managers, designers and developers + Participate in stand ups, plannings, backlog sessions etc. for team’s projects + Be responsible for signing off the feature to be released + Analyse features during the design and development process to point out weaknesses or ambiguities + Create test plans and overall be responsible for test management + Review stories and create bugs when needed * Be a quality advocate on the team ensuring quality is a responsibility for all members of the team * Never stop learning, pursue latest technologies, seek for best solutions * Challenge the norm and won’t be afraid to voice your opinions * Join a group of highly skilled QA engineers continuously looking to improve the quality strategy across the org. ****Who You Are:**** ****What We’re Looking For:**** * 2\+ years experience in software quality assurance * Excellent manual testing skills and out\-of\-the\-box ad hoc testing skills. * Experience in writing clear, concise and comprehensive test plans and test cases * Experience with Agile development methodologies and working in a cross\-functional team environment * Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills * Curious mindset \& detail oriented **Nice To Have:** * An ISTQB® Foundation Level certificate * Automation testing experience * Experience using Cypress and/or Playwright to write automated tests * Experience with Looker CI features and validators a bonus * Knowledge of CI/CD process and how testing can be brought in there * Github experience * Performance and security testing experience or interest * Experience working in the salon or beauty industry **Benefits** 🧘 Your wellbeing is important to us \- we provide private healthcare, 2 Wellness Days, an employee assistance program and a free online GP service. 💰 As part of our Financial Wellbeing, we provide competitive Compensation, an Employee Share Purchase Scheme, Pension, Life Assurance, and Income Protection. 🚵🏿 We help you travel by providing a bike to work scheme as well as tax saver transport tickets. 🦸‍♀️ We support the women who work in Phorest by offering 2 weeks leave for Fertility Treatment, Pregnancy Loss and Menopause. 🍼 We care for your family and provide Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Benefits. 🌳 We grow our own timber! We provide a great learning environment and extensive development opportunities. We run development programs and provide access to many online resources including LinkedIn learning. 🏠 Moving house? Phorest employees get 3 moving days. Want to learn more about Phorest? Check out nothingventured.rocks for our blog and Insights on building an evergreen company from the team here at Phorest. **Phorest is an equal opportunity employer.** For this position, flexi\-time and working from home is possible. We are also open to remote work. Get in touch to ask for more information or to chat about your future with Phorest! *Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised folks tend to only apply when they check every box. So if you think you have what it takes, but don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit.*

title: QC Analyst in Analytical Testing
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met An amazing opportunity has arisen for a QC Analyst in Analytical Testing for one of our clients our new state of the Art single use Multiproduct Biotech facility. What you will do: Bring energy, knowledge, innovation and influence to carry out the following: * Perform a variety of routine Analytical techniques and associated documentation; including but not limited to Capillary Electrophoresis, UPLC, HPLC, GC, Wet Chemistry methods in compliance with GMP requirements * Support activities including general lab readiness, audit readiness, laboratory equipment qualification and analytical method technical transfer, verification and validation * Drive the development of technical skills through coaching and motivate a high performing culture across the team * Foster a continuous improvement environment and bring strong problem solving and troubleshooting capabilities * Consistently deliver on specific area Key Performance Indicators * Operate to the highest standards of Safety, Quality and Compliance * Demonstrate a commitment to learning and personal development that supports Quality,EHS, L\&D and continuous improvement **Requirements** What skills you will need: In order to excel in this role, you will more than likely have: * Degree qualification (Science/Quality/Technical) * 2\-3 years’ experience in the biotechnology and/or pharmaceutical industry, particularly in relation to Capillary Electrophoresis, UPLC, HPLC or GC testing * Preferably Lean Six Sigma experience * Ability to think logically and be proactive under pressure * Ability to work as part of a team and on own initiative in a constructive manner * Flexible and self\-motivated

title: Quality Assurance Engineer
description: **Tech that takes you places** We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. As a QA Engineer, you will help drive the delivery of high\-quality, reliable software across front\-end and back\-end systems. Working within a collaborative Agile team, you will design and execute manual test plans, identify defects and risks, and champion quality throughout the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC). You will work closely with engineers, product managers, designers, and operational teams to ensure quality is embedded from requirements through to release. This role requires strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and a proactive mindset focused on continuous improvement and delivering an excellent customer experience. **Your Journey starts here!** **What you’ll do as a QAEngineer** * Collaborate with Project Managers, Engineers, Product Managers, and Designers to ensure quality is embedded throughout the software development lifecycle. * Analyse business and technical requirements from multiple stakeholder perspectives, including end\-users, partners, operations, and customer support teams. * Design, develop, and execute detailed manual test cases, test scenarios, and regression test plans. * Support the development and maintenance of automated tests to improve software quality, regression coverage, and release confidence. * Identify, document, prioritise, and track defects through to resolution using tools such as Jira. * Support bug triage, escalation management, and root cause analysis activities. * Assess delivery risk and determine appropriate testing scope, coverage, and priorities. * Validate functional and non\-functional requirements across front\-end and back\-end systems. * Monitor and report on testing progress, quality metrics, risks, issues, and dependencies to relevant stakeholders. * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to ensure timely and high\-quality product releases. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across QA processes, standards, methodologies, and tooling. * Support knowledge sharing and mentor junior QA team members where appropriate. * Advocate for best practices in software quality, usability, and customer experience. * Operate effectively within a fast\-paced, collaborative Agile environment. **What you’ll bring to the team** * 3\+ years of experience in Software Quality Assurance or a related testing role. * ISTQB Foundation Certification or equivalent software testing certification is desirable. * Experience working within Agile/Scrum or iterative software development environments. * Strong understanding of software testing principles, methodologies, and defect management processes. * Experience creating and executing manual test plans, test cases, and regression testing activities. * Experience developing, executing, and maintaining automated test suites using modern test automation frameworks and tools. * Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Confluence, Excel, or similar QA/project management tools. * Experience testing web applications, APIs, and back\-end services is advantageous. * Ability to analyse complex requirements and identify potential risks and edge cases. * Exposure to test automation frameworks or AI\-assisted testing tools is advantageous. * Understanding of modern QA practices including
